jobs.title: Senior Associate, Portfolio Management
jobs.description:
Firm Overview: 

Star Mountain is a rapidly growing, specialized private investment firm focused on the large and less competitive lower middle-market (“LMM”), comprised of businesses that generally have between $5 million and $50 million in annual EBITDA. Star Mountain employs three complementary strategies: (i) private credit, (ii) private equity and (iii) secondary private credit and private equity fund investing, all focused within the U.S. LMM. Star Mountain’s track record, business model, growth trajectory and award-winning culture provide compelling career opportunities for driven and talented individuals. Culture is a core principle which includes long-term alignment of interest with 100% of U.S. employees participating in investment profits. Star Mountain has ~$4.5 billion in assets under management (committed capital including debt facilities as of 10/31/25) and expects strong continued growth based on its performance, distinctive investment approach and its diversified investor base of domestic and global institutions (including public pensions, corporate pensions, insurance companies, commercial banks, endowments, foundations and family offices) and individuals. Star Mountain values team players with positive, solutions-based attitudes complemented by a strong work ethic and is heavily committed to long-term training and career development. Star Mountain is employee-owned with material long-term wealth creation opportunity for dedicated, entrepreneurial professionals. Dynamic team of 120+ full-time people and 40+ Senior Advisors / Operating Partners. The senior team has an average of 30 years of investment experience including prior leadership experience at Blackstone / GSO, Fortress, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan and Merrill Lynch.

Position Description:

Portfolio Company Oversight

  • Assist senior Underwriting and Portfolio Management professionals in managing portfolio company relationships and preparing for board meetings, including direct support on operational reviews, performance improvement initiatives, and management accountability tracking
  • Review and analyze monthly financials, quarterly covenant compliance certifications, annual audited financial statements from portfolio companies, with a focus on identifying emerging risks, liquidity constraints, and operational inefficiencies
  • Analyze company-level operating metrics and financials data to disaggregate drivers of under/outperformance relative to budget and prior year, developing actionable insights to support margin improvement, cost optimization, and revenue enhancement initiatives
  • Support value creation initiatives (i.e., Getting Middle Market Ready, working capital management, and reporting, ), including operational benchmarking, KPI enhancement, and execution tracking across portfolio companies
  • Provide analytical and operational support during periods of underperformance or disruption, including liquidity stress, covenant pressure, management transitions, or macro-driven volatility (light crisis management)
  • Assist in evaluating and supporting restructuring, reorganization, and turnaround initiatives, including capital structure optimization, cost rationalization efforts, and strategic realignment analyses.

Internal Reporting

  • Maintain historical portfolio company financial, capital structure and qualitative attributes in firm data management platforms (S&P Global iLEVEL and WSO), ensuring data accuracy to support operational decision-making and performance diagnostics
  • Support valuations processes on portfolio company securities on a quarterly basis, including scenario and downside analyses reflecting operational and restructuring considerations
  • Prepare quarterly and semi-annual credit and equity reviews on individual portfolio companies, highlighting operational trends, execution risks, and value creation progress
  • Craft thoughtful responses to investor requests related to portfolio company security details and performance, including explanations of operational initiatives, performance remediation plans, and restructuring outcomes where applicable

Portfolio Financing and Amendment Support

  • Support drafting and presentation of internal amendment and financing memoranda for relevant approvals from Chief Credit Officer and Investment Committee.
  • Prepare detailed scenario-based spreadsheet models to project portfolio company operating and financial performance, including downside, stress-case, and restructuring scenarios, as well as credit statistics and projected returns
  • Assist investment analysts in completing pre-investment and post-close workflows, procedures and documentation, with added involvement in post-close performance monitoring, amendment execution, and reorganization processes
  • Support cross-functional coordination with legal, restructuring advisors, consultants, and portfolio company management teams during amendments, workouts, and operational improvement initiatives

Required Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of investment-related (private equity, private credit), corporate banking, or other finance-related experience (investment banking, accounting, audit, consulting), with exposure to portfolio monitoring, operational analysis, restructurings, or performance improvement initiatives preferred
  • Microsoft Excel power user, ability to build 3 statement LBO models, including experience with scenario modeling, sensitivity analysis, and operational KPIs
  • Excellent organizational skills with a proactive and detail-oriented approach to managing multiple tasks and priorities
  • Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills
  • Team player with a positive attitude and great people skills

Timing: Immediate

Location: Tampa, Florida

Compensation:

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Benefits:

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits.

Please submit resume through LinkedIn. If we require any additional information, we will reach out directly.

jobs.title: 2025-2026 Private Equity Intern
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP - led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investments on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is currently seeking penultimate and final year undergraduate students or students enrolled in a master’s degree with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an intern position on StepStone’s Private Equity Team. StepStone offers Interns a unique opportunity to achieve a valuable learning experience working across the private equity industry. Through analysis of private equity funds and investments, Interns gain exposure to a wide array of industries and capital structures, and become accustomed to a variety of private equity funds and investment strategies.

What you’ll do

Interns will work in a team environment to target and perform due diligence, including financial modelling and preparation of investment memorandums, on opportunities across the Private Equity team’s three investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investment transactions and secondaries transactions.

Key responsibilities

  • Analyze and conduct diligence on co-investment opportunities and secondary transactions
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree, expected to graduate in 2026 or later
  • Completed at least one relevant 3- or 6-month internship, and able to work on-site at StepStone’s Beijing office at least four days per week 
  • High proficiency in MS Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Flexibility-StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Foreign language skills (desirable)

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in Private Equity.

Position overview:

The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices).

Note: This role is designed for students completing their undergraduate degree who are seeking their first full-time position in Private Equity. This role is part of our full-time analyst program, First STEP, and offers comprehensive training for early-career professionals.

Essential job functions:

  • Work in a team environment with other investment team members to source, screen, and conduct due diligence on selected private equity fund managers, co-investments, and secondaries
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Analyze PPMs, financial statements, and investment returns
  • Conduct reference calls as a part of manager and asset due diligence
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Prepare due diligence materials and Investment Committee memos
  • Present recommendations to the investment team and the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Monitor the performance of the Firm’s existing investments

Education and/or work experience requirements:

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • Undergraduate student graduating anywhere from December 2025 through June 2026
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

Required knowledge, skills, and abilities:

  • Excellent quantitative skills, including financial modeling, analytical and valuation skills for purpose of evaluating investment opportunities
  • Strong sense of risk/reward trade-offs, and an ability to display sound investment judgement
  • Self-starter, with strong ability to think creatively and conduct research to find solutions
  • Ability to communicate and present ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing
  • Capable of multi-tasking with strong attention to detail
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude, with desire to work on various projects

jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:

The Analyst / Associate at StepStone Infrastructure & Real Assets will be responsible for providing support across transactional and Responsible Investment (RI/ESG) activity to SIRA’s local and international team in advising clients on investments in infrastructure and associated real asset sectors globally. The role will require analytical and modelling skills, but the candidate is also expected to have a strong understanding of sustainability concepts and issues.

Essential Job Functions:
  • Strong financial modelling and analytical skills – ability to create and/or modify sophisticated financial models, analyze financial statements and financial forecasts, prepare valuation bridges, run discounted cash flow analysis, comparable public companies, and create sensitivity/scenario analysis;
  • Strong memo / presentation writing and preparation skills;
  • Strong client interaction skills – ability to interact with clients and prepare advisory materials as required;
  • Support investment teams on Responsible Investment, including engaging on ESG risks and opportunities during due diligence and developing and delivering training on key issues to colleagues;
  • Develop RI-related materials, including customized presentations for investor meetings, RFP responses, ESG reporting, and legal documentation
  • Ability to undertake research regarding, and diligence of, potential investment opportunities and sectors, including primary funds, secondaries, co-investments and direct investments;
  • Ability to construct an argument around investment decisions and effectively communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend your position in a team environment, including in Investment Committee meetings;
  • Monitor global ESG trends and emerging best practices (e.g., industry and regulatory updates etc.), providing insights to leadership and identifying and executing on enhancements to the firm’s ESG practices

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 2–5 years of experience in IB, project finance advisory, transaction services, or equivalent with some relevant experience in Responsible Investment (ESG)
  • Relevant buyside and Responsible Investment experience at a FoF or GP would also be considered directly transferable
  • Transaction experience — M&A, financings, or diligence processes with clear deal exposure
  • Ability to produce high-quality (at or near native standard) written content in business English
  • Second European language is preferred (French, German, Danish)
  • Self-starter, eager to learn and desire to grow within the organization
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced global team environment with an ability to manage changing and competing priorities

Other Desired Skills/Attributes:

  • Flexibility – StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Evidence of ESG certification (e.g. CFA Sustainable Investing, IFRS / SASB FSA credential) is a plus, but not required

jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:         

StepStone Private Wealth Solutions is seeking an Analyst/Associate with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills to support the product management efforts of financial platforms globally as they consider the suite of funds under management. The team oversees and manages data across five portfolio strategies and controls the information being shared externally.

The Analyst/Associate’s primary role is to draft high-quality, consistent responses, fund materials, and other analyses in response to requests from current and prospective platforms. The Analyst/Associate will work closely with the existing members of the product management team as they learn the process and expectations with the opportunity to begin collaborating with and have exposure to all levels across the firm. The candidate is expected to show a willingness to learn the underlying products and the comparable product landscape to support these efforts appropriately. The person should also have a basic understanding and interest in the private markets investment space (specifically, private equity, infrastructure, real estate, and private debt), strong interpersonal skills and project management skills. The candidate should be highly analytical and naturally curious to understand the underlying data. Strong written communication, organization, attention to detail and relationship building skills will be necessary to succeed in this role.         

Essential Job Functions: 

  • Responsible for completing written responses to inquiries from prospective platforms and existing clients, including writing and editing content, working with cross-functional teams to obtain the necessary information, and ensuring that the final product is accurate and delivered on time.
  • Critically assess the accuracy and relevance of content provided by cross-functional team members in response to DDQs/RFPs.
  • Contribute to the refinement and implementation of internal AI initiatives designed to pre-populate and streamline questionnaire responses
  • Manage and maintain the private wealth central database of responses to ensure content is kept up to date.
  • Respond to ad hoc requests initiated by platforms seeking deeper analysis of performance metrics, portfolio construction, or underlying investments.
  • Collaborate with portfolio managers, BD leadership, investment, legal, compliance, fund accounting, and portfolio analytics teams to synthesize insights across performance drivers, capital deployment dynamics, fundraising trends, portfolio evolution, and competitive positioning.
  • Maintain a high standard of data integrity and presentation quality across all portfolio-related deliverables.
  • Contribute to the development of portfolio narratives and reusable content for quarterly updates and platform communications.
  • Assist with various projects to streamline internal processes.
  • Strong ability to focus on the task at hand with an attention of the final deadline associated with the project or deliverable.

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:         

  • Undergraduate degree / higher education qualification in finance, business, data science, or liberal arts.
  • Experience with related financial services (private equity, investment banking, asset management, investor relations and/or consulting) is a plus.
  • Highly motivated self-starter, with demonstrated history of taking initiative, being adaptive in a fast-changing environment, and working independently.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:          

  • Candidate should be an energetic, self-motivated, and team-oriented individual who thrives in high-performance environments and maintains composure and precision under tight deadlines.
  • Demonstrated intellectual curiosity and commitment to developing deep private markets expertise.
  • Demonstrated interest in financial technology, automation, and AI-enabled workflow optimization.
  • Excellent report writing and communication skills.
  •  High level of confidentiality and understanding regulatory boundaries with sharing information.
  • Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to be flexible with changing priorities and company growth.
  • Ability to take ownership and accountability.

Position Overview

The Distribution & Sales Enablement Analyst serves as a strategic partner to the Private Wealth team and is responsible for planning, coordinating, and optimizing conferences, events, and broader distribution and marketing initiatives that support advisor engagement and other business goals.

This role owns key elements of strategy, execution and processes—anticipating needs and ensuring that distribution efforts are delivered with consistency, quality, and strategic intent. The Analyst brings structure and forward planning, enabling sales teams to focus on relationship‑building and growth.

Essential Job Functions

  • Lead key initiatives and strategic priorities that support Private Wealth’s distribution objectives, sales and engagement priorities, and marketing initiatives
  • Own the external conference and event strategy and related execution for Private Wealth, including calendar planning and cross‑functional coordination.
  • Serve as a strategic partner to Sales leadership to ensure conference participation and events are aligned with distribution objectives, target audiences, and coverage priorities.
  • Collaborate with Marketing to ensure strategic alignment of messaging, collateral, and event materials with broader brand and distribution strategies.
  • Lead end‑to‑end management of conferences, including timelines, deliverables, and communication.
  • Oversee event platforms, portals, registrations, attendee data, exhibitor requirements, vendors, and lead‑capture processes, ensuring accuracy and timely execution.
  • Oversee logistics such as booth strategy, signage, shipping, and branded materials with an emphasis on consistency, quality, and efficiency.
  • Support speaker engagement and program coordination
  • Direct StepStone‑hosted and co‑hosted distribution events, including advisor dinners, and small‑group engagements.
  • Participate in internal sales meetings and due diligence sessions by directing sales enablement frameworks, including materials, logistics, strategy, and timelines.
  • Act as the primary point of contact with conference organizers, venues, and third‑party vendors, managing relationships, selecting vendors, negotiating contract terms, and resolving issues independently.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • 2–5+ years of experience in sales enablement, distribution support, marketing operations, events, or sponsorship management; experience in asset management or financial services preferred.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Ability to operate independently, manage competing priorities, and drive initiatives from planning through execution.
  • Strong organizational, analytical and project management skills, with comfort navigating event platforms, CRMs, spreadsheets, and project‑tracking tools.
  • Proven ability to collaborate across functions and communicate effectively.
  • Detail‑oriented, solutions‑focused, and comfortable exercising judgment in a dynamic environment.
  • Willingness to travel for key distribution events.
  • Strong time management and organizational skills.

jobs.title: Analyst - Infrastructure Private Equity Investments
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:

The Analyst at StepStone Infrastructure & Real Assets will be responsible for providing analytical, modeling, and transactional support to SIRA’s local and international team in advising clients on investments in infrastructure and associated real asset sectors globally.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Strong financial modelling and analytical skills – ability to create and/or modify sophisticated financial models, analyze financial statements and financial forecasts, prepare valuation bridges, run discounted cash flow analysis, comparable public companies, and create sensitivity/scenario analysis;
  • Strong memo / presentation writing and preparation skills;
  • Ability to undertake research regarding, and diligence of, potential investment opportunities and sectors, including primary funds, secondaries, co-investments and direct investments;
  • Ability to construct an argument around investment decisions and effectively communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend your position in a team environment, including in Investment Committee meetings;
  • Strong client interaction skills – ability to interact with clients and prepare advisory materials as required; and,
  • Support senior staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 1–3 years of experience in IB, project finance advisory, transaction services, or equivalent
  • Relevant buyside experience at a FoF or GP would also be considered directly transferable
  • Transaction experience — M&A, financings, or diligence processes with clear deal exposure
  • Ability to produce high-quality (at or near native standard) written content in business English
  • Second European language is preferred (French, German, Danish)
  • Self-starter, eager to learn and desire to grow within the organization
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced global team environment with an ability to manage changing and competing priorities

Other Desired Skills/Attributes:

  • Flexibility – StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • German, Italian, French language skills a plus

At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success.  We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to create an environment that empowers our employees and allows them to be heard, regardless of title or tenure.  Our organizational community features multiple Employment Resource Groups as well as mentorship programs to enhance the employee experience for all.  

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, StepStone does not discriminate on the basis of race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by law.

Candidates must be at least 18 years old to apply.

jobs.title: Analyst - Private Equity - Primaries/Co-Investments
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

An Analyst of StepStone Private Equity in London will be focused on supporting the local Private Equity team in its investment activities and will be active across two investment types (primaries and co-investments in a buyout, venture or growth equity setting).

Essential Job Functions:

  • Develop a basic understanding of the private equity landscape and industry trends
  • Interpret financial analyses, prepare valuation analyses and use available information to identify, quantify and analyze trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of an investment opportunity and efficiently communicate that opinion both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings
  • Support Senior staff on sourcing and managing the investment processes

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • BA/BS in Business, Finance, Math, Engineering, Economics or Accounting, or equivalent relevant
  • Demonstrated track record of academic and professional success
  • 1-3 years in Investment Banking or Consulting

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Leadership potential
  • High proficiency in Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • European language skills (desirable)

jobs.title: Associate / Senior Associate - Product Management & Investor Relations, Infrastructure & Real Assets
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview
The Associate / Senior Associate will be an integral member of StepStone Enterprise Services’ Product Management team. The role will support StepStone’s infrastructure and real assets platform investing in co-investment, secondary and primary infrastructure and real asset transactions globally. Responsibilities will consist of providing support to senior specialists for infrastructure and real asset products across the entire fund lifecycle from fund launches and capital formation to existing investor relations activities, including helping to create new content, responding to investor requests, and supporting investor roadshows/events. Periodic interactions with limited partners and potential investors may also be required.
Essential Job Functions:
  • Maintain and implement client service protocols to provide a high quality, consistent investor experience, including the application of standardized best practices gleaned from across the platform
  • Compile and write content for investor communications and drafting external and internal speaking points
  • Create and maintain marketing materials including presentations, fact cards, PPMs, investor updates, and other documents related to private investment fundraises
  • Prepare and manage existing and prospective investor requests, including responding to RFPs, DDQs, other due diligence requests, and providing updates; collaborate with internal groups for responses as needed and ensure answers to clients are accurate, consistent, and appropriate
  • Build collaborative and productive relationship with multiple stakeholders, including but not limited to, Global Business Development team members, Infrastructure and Real Assets investment professionals, Legal & Compliance, Finance and Accounting, and StepStone Portfolio Analytics and Reporting team to execute on investor relations processes and fundraising strategy
  • Understand and analyze infrastructure industry trends and conduct asset industry research
  • Coordination and logistics of client events, onsite meetings, webinar events, investor conferences
  • Coordinate with technology to enforce internal efficiencies
  • Help support and execute on key initiatives as needed
Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:
  • Bachelor’s degree required in finance, accounting, economics or business preferred
  • 5 or more years of infrastructure and real assets or financial services experience is required
  • Investor relations, analytical, and presentation experience strongly preferred
  • Experience at a private markets asset manager, a private markets placement firm, a financial services or investment bank is preferred
Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
  • Strong sense of ethics and integrity with demonstrated ability to behave consistently with StepStone’s expressed values and principles
  • Demonstrated ability to work effectively as part of a team with collegiality and a high level of professionalism
  • Excellent technical skills, including ability to learn and become the subject matter expert in the products to respond to investor requests

jobs.title: Fund Controller - Collateralized Fund Obligation & Structured Products
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

The Fund Controller, Fund Accounting – CFO & Structured Products will be responsible for accounting, financial reporting, and assist with operational execution for Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) products, other structured credit vehicles, and Private Equity vehicles.  The Fund Controller acts as the internal subject‑matter expert on CFO structures, bridging Fund Accounting, Treasury, Investment Operations, Capital Markets, Legal, Tax, external auditors, trustees, collateral administrators, and rating agencies. The role is designed to reduce execution risk, internalize institutional knowledge, and establish repeatable operating and accounting standards for a growing structured products platform. 

In this position, the Fund Controller will manage a team of accountants and various fund administrators. The Fund Controller will also work closely with the Fund Accounting Treasury and Operations teams along with the Investment Operations team.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Own the end‑to‑end fund accounting and financial reporting framework for CFO vehicles, including master/feeder structures, note issuances, equity tranches, and related SPVs.
  • Serve as an internal expert matter on indenture interpretation and accounting implications, partnering with Legal and auditors on complex or judgmental matters.
  • Review and challenge work prepared by collateral administrators, trustees, and third‑party fund administrators, ensuring outputs align with governing documents and accounting standards.
  • Act as the designated CFO product lead for launches and closings, coordinating high‑volume cash activity and multi‑party workflows
  • Oversee funding mechanics, drawdowns, note interest calculations, waterfall allocations, and reserve accounts.
  • Additionally, manage and oversee a portfolio of Private Equity funds, which includes but is not limited to:
  • Review work prepared by the fund administrator
    • Reviewing accounting entries from the Fund Administrator’s accounting and reporting systems  
    • Reviewing and reconciling cash and investment accounts with General Partner reports  
    • Reviewing of investment partnership reports
    • Reviewing and reconciliation of capital account activity
    • Review quarterly unaudited and annual audited financial statements in accordance with GAAP
    • Coordinate preparation of annual audits with outside CPA firm
    • Review partner allocations and capital account statements
    • Manage process for delivering quarterly and annual investor reports and other ad-hoc communications
  • Understand partnership agreements and interpret reporting requirements and partner economics
  • Review management fees, distribution waterfalls and carried interest for Private Equity vehicles
  • Corresponding with General Partners regarding accounting/reporting questions, or other issues, as necessary    
  • Corresponding with underlying investors regarding questions, requests, etc.
  • Respond to investor requests in a timely manner
  • Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate
  • Perform other duties as required, including but not limited to:
    • Review of K-1s and support for tax reporting and analysis of tax matters
    • Support for the Compliance team, including preparation of materials for ADV, Form PF, SLT, etc.
    • Support for other departments across StepStone

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • Experience with accounting for structured finance, private equity partnership, and fund of funds
  • Direct experience with CFOs, CLOs, NAV‑based facilities, or other structured credit products
  • Deep understanding of fund structures, note issuances, waterfalls, and cash flow mechanics
  • A Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance with 12+ years of professional industry experience
  • Ability to multi-task and manage priorities against fast-paced key deadlines
  • Ideal candidates will have experience with SEC registered investment advisors
  • Must be able to work independently but contribute in a team-oriented environment
  • Previous experience with Investment Company Accounting and ASC 820 (FAS 157) and FIN 48
  • Strong Microsoft Excel experience
  • Experience training and managing junior professionals and reviewing the work of others

Other Attributes: 

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to maintain strict confidentiality of client, investment and company information
  • Ability to problem solve and leverage technology
  • Acute attention to accuracy and detail
  • Professional demeanor and an ability to work well in a team environment
  • Highly proficient in using Excel, Word, and other Microsoft office programs
  • Willingness and ability to learn and implement new programs and processes

Salary Range: $160,000 - $180,000

The salary range is an estimate of pay for this position.  Actual pay may vary depending on job-related factors that can include location, education, skill, and experience.  The salary range does not include any benefits or other forms of possible compensation that may be available to employees.

jobs.title: Intern - Real Estate Investments
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The Intern will join StepStone’s Real Estate Team in Beijing. StepStone offers Interns an opportunity to achieve valuable learning experiences working across the real estate industry. The Intern will work in a team environment to evaluate opportunities across real estate private fund investments, co-investments, and secondary investments across the Asia Pacific region. The Intern will gain exposure to a wide array of property types, markets and capital structures and become accustomed to a variety of real estate private equity funds and investment strategies.

Essential Internship Functions

  • Assist both the advisory and investment teams in key job functions, primarily:
    • The underwriting and assessment of prospective fund managers on behalf of our advisory capital
    • The evaluation of potential investments and the asset management of the existing real estate portfolio
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of property types
  • Interpret financial analyses prepared by operating fund managers, including investment track records, financial statement analysis and fund models
  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with fund managers and assist in all aspects of active investment and advisory ongoing due diligence
  • Efficiently communicate a position verbally and in written materials, including defending a position in a team environment such as Investment Committee meetings

Timing, Location

  • Immediate ability to start working at StepStone, ideally for six months (minimum three months).
  • The job is based in Beijing and presence in the office is required for the majority of the days.

Qualifications

  • You are a Junior/Rising Senior committed to or graduated with BA/BS in Finance, Economics or Accounting, or equivalent relevant experience
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English
  • High proficiency in MS Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Demonstrated track record of academic success
  • Team player and strong work ethic
  • Pointed interest in the investment world, Real Estate is a plus
  • R.C work authorization

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

POSITION OVERVIEW:   
             
The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices). 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Work in a team environment with other investment team members to source, screen, and conduct due diligence on select private equity fund managers, co-investments, and secondaries
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Analyze PPMs, financial statements, and investment returns
  • Conduct reference calls as a part of manager and asset due diligence
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Prepare due diligence materials and Investment Committee memos
  • Present recommendations to the investment team and the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Monitor the performance of the Firm’s existing investments

EDUCATION AND/OR WORK EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • 1 year of investment banking, consulting, and/or private equity experience   
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES: 

  • Excellent quantitative skills, including financial modeling, analytical and valuation skills for purpose of evaluating investment opportunities
  • Strong sense of risk/reward trade-offs, and an ability to display sound investment judgement
  • Self-starter, with strong ability to think creatively and conduct research to find solutions
  • Ability to communicate and present ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing
  • Capable of multi-tasking with strong attention to detail
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude, with desire to work on various projects
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Fluent (written and spoken) English

Salary Range: $105,000 - $112,500

The salary range is an estimate of pay for this position.  Actual pay may vary depending on job-related factors that can include location, education, skill, and experience.  The salary range does not include any benefits or other forms of possible compensation that may be available to employees.

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

About StepStone:

StepStone (Nasdaq: STEP) is a global private markets specialist overseeing over US$723 billion of private capital allocations, including approximately US$199 billion of assets under management.

StepStone Private Equity provides its investors and clients with customized exposure to private equity through its global market relationships, deep research capabilities, granular due diligence, and structuring capabilities. StepStone builds this exposure through its commingled funds, customized funds, separately managed accounts, and investment advisory assignments.

Position Overview:

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an Analyst position on StepStone’s Private Equity Investment Team in the Firm’s Beijing or Singapore office. The Analyst’s primary role is to engage in sector-focused research on various areas of the private equity industry such as venture capital, growth, buyout and distressed strategies in Asia. In addition, the Analyst will work in a team environment with other investment team members to target and perform due diligence on selected private companies and private equity funds; analyze PPMs, financial statements and investment returns; and conduct reference calls and personal interviews. The Analyst will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Firm’s Investment Committee.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and 1+ years of private equity, investment banking, consulting, or other relevant experience. The Analyst will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability, and attentiveness to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

· Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

· Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including investment track records and financial statement analysis; prepare valuation analyses on private companies using comparable public companies and precedent transactions

· Support senior research staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities

· Assess marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and important areas for further investigation

· Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity

· Construct an argument around an opinion and efficiently communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings

· Use creativity to source and analyze data and to test hypotheses regarding attractiveness of specific sectors and investment opportunities

· Analyze and monitor the performance of the Firm's existing fund managers and underlying portfolio companies; prepare for and participate in meetings with assigned managers, and conduct on-going due diligence

Other Requirements:

· BA/BS from a leading university; top quartile academic background; demonstrated track record of academic and professional success

· Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications

· Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills

· Leadership potential

· High proficiency in Word, Power Point and Excel

· Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop

· Travel around 5 days per month to regions throughout Asia (Greater China, Korea, Japan, India, Australia, SE Asia, etc.

jobs.title: Private Wealth Operations Manager
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

StepStone is looking for a customer-centric, detail-oriented individual who will work with a team that manages fund operations and client relations for the StepStone Australia Funds. The person should have a solid understanding of investment operations and strong communication skills.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Manage daily communication with the fund’s Australia transfer agent and administrator to ensure a seamless client experience.
  • Coordinate with the fund’s Responsible Entity (RE) to resolve operational matters efficiently and maintain a strong Administrator–RE relationship.
  • Partner with the Australia Business Development team to understand priorities and support distribution initiatives.
  • Build and maintain a strong working relationship with the EMEA Operations team to ensure accurate trade processing.
  • Collaborate with the legal team to update documentation and ensure timely approval and implementation.
  • Work with the due diligence team to complete information requests promptly and accurately.
  • Develop and maintain quarterly KPIs for both external service providers and internal performance metrics.
  • Maintain a project tracker for open items and ensure timely updates across teams.
  • Prepare distribution updates and investor resolutions for international board materials.
  • Ensure all data providers and pricing sources receive accurate and up‑to‑date information.
  • Support leadership in managing service providers, distribution platforms, and other external vendors.

Position Overview:

The Senior Fund Accountant will support the day-to-day fund accounting, valuation, analysis and oversight of US and non-US Funds. The Senior Fund Accountant will work closely with StepStone Private Wealth Fund Accounting and StepStone Group Monitoring and Reporting teams.

Essential Job Functions:

This hands-on, detail-oriented position will:

  • Track daily investment activity, including funding investments and overseeing investment related expenses
  • Managing investment portfolio forecasts, GAAP, accruals and comparison to actuals
  • Provide assistance with updates to portfolio management data (SPI) based on daily reconciliations
  • Assist in the preparation of monthly Valuation Committee materials

  • Track daily cash activity, including prompting administrator wires and overseeing invoice/expense payments
  • Managing fund forecasts and comparing to actuals
  • Reconcile foreign exchange hedge positions for accuracy, timely break resolution, and compliance with fund accounting policies
  • Support various fund administrators and coordinate delivery of required reporting, etc.
  • Respond to sub-adviser and team member requests in a professional and timely manner
  • Assist with daily work associated with accounting and reporting functions
  • Assist with preparation of company dashboards and board decks
  • Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate

  • Three to five years of accounting or audit experience
  • Previous experience with Investment Company Accounting and ASC 820 (FAS 157) preferred

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Strong Microsoft Excel experience

jobs.title: 2025-2026 Private Equity Intern
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP - led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investments on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is currently seeking penultimate and final year undergraduate students or students enrolled in a master’s degree with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an intern position on StepStone’s Private Equity Team. StepStone offers Interns a unique opportunity to achieve a valuable learning experience working across the private equity industry. Through analysis of private equity funds and investments, Interns gain exposure to a wide array of industries and capital structures, and become accustomed to a variety of private equity funds and investment strategies.

What you’ll do

Interns will work in a team environment to target and perform due diligence, including financial modelling and preparation of investment memorandums, on opportunities across the Private Equity team’s three investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investment transactions and secondaries transactions.

Key responsibilities

  • Analyze and conduct diligence on co-investment opportunities and secondary transactions
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree, expected to graduate in 2026 or later
  • Completed at least one relevant 3- or 6-month internship, and able to work on-site at StepStone’s Beijing office at least four days per week 
  • High proficiency in MS Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Flexibility-StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Foreign language skills (desirable)

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in Private Equity.

The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices).

Note: This role is designed for students completing their undergraduate degree who are seeking their first full-time position in Private Equity. This role is part of our full-time analyst program, First STEP, and offers comprehensive training for early-career professionals.

jobs.title: Analyst / Associate, Infrastructure Private Equity / Responsible Investment
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:

The Analyst / Associate at StepStone Infrastructure & Real Assets will be responsible for providing support across transactional and Responsible Investment (RI/ESG) activity to SIRA’s local and international team in advising clients on investments in infrastructure and associated real asset sectors globally. The role will require analytical and modelling skills, but the candidate is also expected to have a strong understanding of sustainability concepts and issues.

Essential Job Functions:
  • Strong financial modelling and analytical skills – ability to create and/or modify sophisticated financial models, analyze financial statements and financial forecasts, prepare valuation bridges, run discounted cash flow analysis, comparable public companies, and create sensitivity/scenario analysis;
  • Strong memo / presentation writing and preparation skills;
  • Strong client interaction skills – ability to interact with clients and prepare advisory materials as required;
  • Support investment teams on Responsible Investment, including engaging on ESG risks and opportunities during due diligence and developing and delivering training on key issues to colleagues;
  • Develop RI-related materials, including customized presentations for investor meetings, RFP responses, ESG reporting, and legal documentation
  • Ability to undertake research regarding, and diligence of, potential investment opportunities and sectors, including primary funds, secondaries, co-investments and direct investments;
  • Ability to construct an argument around investment decisions and effectively communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend your position in a team environment, including in Investment Committee meetings;
  • Monitor global ESG trends and emerging best practices (e.g., industry and regulatory updates etc.), providing insights to leadership and identifying and executing on enhancements to the firm’s ESG practices

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 2–5 years of experience in IB, project finance advisory, transaction services, or equivalent with some relevant experience in Responsible Investment (ESG)
  • Relevant buyside and Responsible Investment experience at a FoF or GP would also be considered directly transferable
  • Transaction experience — M&A, financings, or diligence processes with clear deal exposure
  • Ability to produce high-quality (at or near native standard) written content in business English
  • Second European language is preferred (French, German, Danish)
  • Self-starter, eager to learn and desire to grow within the organization
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced global team environment with an ability to manage changing and competing priorities

Other Desired Skills/Attributes:

  • Flexibility – StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Evidence of ESG certification (e.g. CFA Sustainable Investing, IFRS / SASB FSA credential) is a plus, but not required

jobs.title: Analyst/Associate Product Management - Private Wealth
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:         

StepStone Private Wealth Solutions is seeking an Analyst/Associate with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills to support the product management efforts of financial platforms globally as they consider the suite of funds under management. The team oversees and manages data across five portfolio strategies and controls the information being shared externally.

The Analyst/Associate’s primary role is to draft high-quality, consistent responses, fund materials, and other analyses in response to requests from current and prospective platforms. The Analyst/Associate will work closely with the existing members of the product management team as they learn the process and expectations with the opportunity to begin collaborating with and have exposure to all levels across the firm. The candidate is expected to show a willingness to learn the underlying products and the comparable product landscape to support these efforts appropriately. The person should also have a basic understanding and interest in the private markets investment space (specifically, private equity, infrastructure, real estate, and private debt), strong interpersonal skills and project management skills. The candidate should be highly analytical and naturally curious to understand the underlying data. Strong written communication, organization, attention to detail and relationship building skills will be necessary to succeed in this role.         

Essential Job Functions: 

  • Responsible for completing written responses to inquiries from prospective platforms and existing clients, including writing and editing content, working with cross-functional teams to obtain the necessary information, and ensuring that the final product is accurate and delivered on time.
  • Critically assess the accuracy and relevance of content provided by cross-functional team members in response to DDQs/RFPs.
  • Contribute to the refinement and implementation of internal AI initiatives designed to pre-populate and streamline questionnaire responses
  • Manage and maintain the private wealth central database of responses to ensure content is kept up to date.
  • Respond to ad hoc requests initiated by platforms seeking deeper analysis of performance metrics, portfolio construction, or underlying investments.
  • Collaborate with portfolio managers, BD leadership, investment, legal, compliance, fund accounting, and portfolio analytics teams to synthesize insights across performance drivers, capital deployment dynamics, fundraising trends, portfolio evolution, and competitive positioning.
  • Maintain a high standard of data integrity and presentation quality across all portfolio-related deliverables.
  • Contribute to the development of portfolio narratives and reusable content for quarterly updates and platform communications.
  • Assist with various projects to streamline internal processes.
  • Strong ability to focus on the task at hand with an attention of the final deadline associated with the project or deliverable.

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:         

  • Undergraduate degree / higher education qualification in finance, business, data science, or liberal arts.
  • Experience with related financial services (private equity, investment banking, asset management, investor relations and/or consulting) is a plus.
  • Highly motivated self-starter, with demonstrated history of taking initiative, being adaptive in a fast-changing environment, and working independently.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:          

  • Candidate should be an energetic, self-motivated, and team-oriented individual who thrives in high-performance environments and maintains composure and precision under tight deadlines.
  • Demonstrated intellectual curiosity and commitment to developing deep private markets expertise.
  • Demonstrated interest in financial technology, automation, and AI-enabled workflow optimization.
  • Excellent report writing and communication skills.
  •  High level of confidentiality and understanding regulatory boundaries with sharing information.
  • Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to be flexible with changing priorities and company growth.
  • Ability to take ownership and accountability.

Position Overview

The Distribution & Sales Enablement Analyst serves as a strategic partner to the Private Wealth team and is responsible for planning, coordinating, and optimizing conferences, events, and broader distribution and marketing initiatives that support advisor engagement and other business goals.

This role owns key elements of strategy, execution and processes—anticipating needs and ensuring that distribution efforts are delivered with consistency, quality, and strategic intent. The Analyst brings structure and forward planning, enabling sales teams to focus on relationship‑building and growth.

Essential Job Functions

  • Lead key initiatives and strategic priorities that support Private Wealth’s distribution objectives, sales and engagement priorities, and marketing initiatives
  • Own the external conference and event strategy and related execution for Private Wealth, including calendar planning and cross‑functional coordination.
  • Serve as a strategic partner to Sales leadership to ensure conference participation and events are aligned with distribution objectives, target audiences, and coverage priorities.
  • Collaborate with Marketing to ensure strategic alignment of messaging, collateral, and event materials with broader brand and distribution strategies.
  • Lead end‑to‑end management of conferences, including timelines, deliverables, and communication.
  • Oversee event platforms, portals, registrations, attendee data, exhibitor requirements, vendors, and lead‑capture processes, ensuring accuracy and timely execution.
  • Oversee logistics such as booth strategy, signage, shipping, and branded materials with an emphasis on consistency, quality, and efficiency.
  • Support speaker engagement and program coordination
  • Direct StepStone‑hosted and co‑hosted distribution events, including advisor dinners, and small‑group engagements.
  • Participate in internal sales meetings and due diligence sessions by directing sales enablement frameworks, including materials, logistics, strategy, and timelines.
  • Act as the primary point of contact with conference organizers, venues, and third‑party vendors, managing relationships, selecting vendors, negotiating contract terms, and resolving issues independently.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • 2–5+ years of experience in sales enablement, distribution support, marketing operations, events, or sponsorship management; experience in asset management or financial services preferred.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Ability to operate independently, manage competing priorities, and drive initiatives from planning through execution.
  • Strong organizational, analytical and project management skills, with comfort navigating event platforms, CRMs, spreadsheets, and project‑tracking tools.
  • Proven ability to collaborate across functions and communicate effectively.
  • Detail‑oriented, solutions‑focused, and comfortable exercising judgment in a dynamic environment.
  • Willingness to travel for key distribution events.
  • Strong time management and organizational skills.

jobs.title: Fund Controller - Collateralized Fund Obligation & Structured Products
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

The Fund Controller, Fund Accounting – CFO & Structured Products will be responsible for accounting, financial reporting, and assist with operational execution for Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) products, other structured credit vehicles, and Private Equity vehicles.  The Fund Controller acts as the internal subject‑matter expert on CFO structures, bridging Fund Accounting, Treasury, Investment Operations, Capital Markets, Legal, Tax, external auditors, trustees, collateral administrators, and rating agencies. The role is designed to reduce execution risk, internalize institutional knowledge, and establish repeatable operating and accounting standards for a growing structured products platform. 

In this position, the Fund Controller will manage a team of accountants and various fund administrators. The Fund Controller will also work closely with the Fund Accounting Treasury and Operations teams along with the Investment Operations team.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Own the end‑to‑end fund accounting and financial reporting framework for CFO vehicles, including master/feeder structures, note issuances, equity tranches, and related SPVs.
  • Serve as an internal expert matter on indenture interpretation and accounting implications, partnering with Legal and auditors on complex or judgmental matters.
  • Review and challenge work prepared by collateral administrators, trustees, and third‑party fund administrators, ensuring outputs align with governing documents and accounting standards.
  • Act as the designated CFO product lead for launches and closings, coordinating high‑volume cash activity and multi‑party workflows
  • Oversee funding mechanics, drawdowns, note interest calculations, waterfall allocations, and reserve accounts.
  • Additionally, manage and oversee a portfolio of Private Equity funds, which includes but is not limited to:
  • Review work prepared by the fund administrator
    • Reviewing accounting entries from the Fund Administrator’s accounting and reporting systems  
    • Reviewing and reconciling cash and investment accounts with General Partner reports  
    • Reviewing of investment partnership reports
    • Reviewing and reconciliation of capital account activity
    • Review quarterly unaudited and annual audited financial statements in accordance with GAAP
    • Coordinate preparation of annual audits with outside CPA firm
    • Review partner allocations and capital account statements
    • Manage process for delivering quarterly and annual investor reports and other ad-hoc communications
  • Understand partnership agreements and interpret reporting requirements and partner economics
  • Review management fees, distribution waterfalls and carried interest for Private Equity vehicles
  • Corresponding with General Partners regarding accounting/reporting questions, or other issues, as necessary    
  • Corresponding with underlying investors regarding questions, requests, etc.
  • Respond to investor requests in a timely manner
  • Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate
  • Perform other duties as required, including but not limited to:
    • Review of K-1s and support for tax reporting and analysis of tax matters
    • Support for the Compliance team, including preparation of materials for ADV, Form PF, SLT, etc.
    • Support for other departments across StepStone

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • Experience with accounting for structured finance, private equity partnership, and fund of funds
  • Direct experience with CFOs, CLOs, NAV‑based facilities, or other structured credit products
  • Deep understanding of fund structures, note issuances, waterfalls, and cash flow mechanics
  • A Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance with 12+ years of professional industry experience
  • Ability to multi-task and manage priorities against fast-paced key deadlines
  • Ideal candidates will have experience with SEC registered investment advisors
  • Must be able to work independently but contribute in a team-oriented environment
  • Previous experience with Investment Company Accounting and ASC 820 (FAS 157) and FIN 48
  • Strong Microsoft Excel experience
  • Experience training and managing junior professionals and reviewing the work of others

Other Attributes: 

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to maintain strict confidentiality of client, investment and company information
  • Ability to problem solve and leverage technology
  • Acute attention to accuracy and detail
  • Professional demeanor and an ability to work well in a team environment
  • Highly proficient in using Excel, Word, and other Microsoft office programs
  • Willingness and ability to learn and implement new programs and processes

Salary Range: $160,000 - $180,000

The salary range is an estimate of pay for this position.  Actual pay may vary depending on job-related factors that can include location, education, skill, and experience.  The salary range does not include any benefits or other forms of possible compensation that may be available to employees.

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

POSITION OVERVIEW:   
             
The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices). 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Work in a team environment with other investment team members to source, screen, and conduct due diligence on select private equity fund managers, co-investments, and secondaries
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Analyze PPMs, financial statements, and investment returns
  • Conduct reference calls as a part of manager and asset due diligence
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Prepare due diligence materials and Investment Committee memos
  • Present recommendations to the investment team and the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Monitor the performance of the Firm’s existing investments

EDUCATION AND/OR WORK EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • 1 year of investment banking, consulting, and/or private equity experience   
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES: 

  • Excellent quantitative skills, including financial modeling, analytical and valuation skills for purpose of evaluating investment opportunities
  • Strong sense of risk/reward trade-offs, and an ability to display sound investment judgement
  • Self-starter, with strong ability to think creatively and conduct research to find solutions
  • Ability to communicate and present ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing
  • Capable of multi-tasking with strong attention to detail
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude, with desire to work on various projects
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Fluent (written and spoken) English

Salary Range: $105,000 - $112,500
