jobs.title: Associate Director, Asset Management
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary:


The Associate Director will be a key member of the ORIX Growth Capital team in New York, focusing on underwriting and portfolio management.  Key elements of the role include company and investment evaluation, industry and economic research, analysis of micro- and macroeconomic trends impacting company performance, term sheet review, company-specific and industry-wide due diligence, financial modeling, legal documentation and close, focusing on underwriting and portfolio management.  The successful candidate will work closely with Senior Management of ORIX Growth Capital, equity investors and management teams to successfully grow the ORIX Growth Capital brand. ORIX Growth Capital is proud of its talented team, strong industry relationships and commitment to long term success. Candidates must have a strong interest in technology companies and a desire to work closely with venture capital and private equity communities.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Perform industry and relevant economic research in order to analyze relevant trends and key risks on investment opportunities
  • Communicate key industry and economic trends impacting current and future company performance both verbally and via written investment memorandums and research reports
  • Evaluate potential investment opportunities and create financial models using company-specific/industry-wide factors and overall short- and long-term economic trends, and discuss relevant impacts to the investment
  • Serve a primary lead in due diligence efforts, including company-specific, industry-wide and macroeconomic risks and opportunities, as well as collecting and analyzing relevant data and trends
  • Prepare term sheets, credit applications, and other documentation required for approval of deal by investment committee and executive management
  • Coordinate legal review and documentation for deal closing
  • Build and nurture ongoing relationships with new and existing clients and investors
  • Work with ORIX Growth Capital portfolio companies, including monitoring of performance, attending board meetings, and following up on investment
  • Some domestic travel required, dependent on investment activities
  • Other duties and projects, as assigned

Contacts

This position has frequent contact with all levels of employees and management. In addition, this role may interact with outside business partners, vendors, consultants, and other office visitors.

Education, Skills and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree from accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required; Master's degree preferred
  • 5+ years of experience at a top-tier investment bank, private equity or debt fund, venture capital firm or growth technology company
  • Deep understanding of software & technology industries, and venture & growth ecosystems
  • Highly developed analytical skills with ability to define issues, collect and analyze pertinent data, identify alternative solutions, project consequences of proposed actions, draw valid conclusions, propose sound recommendations, and implement approved solutions in support of specified goals. Attention to detail is critical
  • Excellent proficiency in accounting and financial modeling
  • Exceptional organizational, communication, and presentation skills, including the ability to prepare comprehensive and insightful investment memoranda
  • Solid interpersonal skills with success as an approachable collaborator at all levels of an organization
  • Demonstrated ability to plan, organize, and prioritize assignments, and to meet critical and established deadlines; Ability to manage multiple changing assignments simultaneously without constant supervision
  • A strong team orientation and the ability to thrive in a fun, performance-driven environment
  • Ability to travel, as needed, for investment activities, sometimes with short notice

Annual base salary gross: $160,000 - $200,000 (CA only).  The base salary range represents the estimated low and high end of ORIX’s salary for this position based on geography. Actual base salaries will vary and will be based on various factors, such as candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and proficiency for the role. The base pay is one component of ORIX’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards and benefits include cash bonuses, long term incentives, health insurance, accident and life insurance, and retirement benefits.  Please visit our Benefits page for additional information.

Life at ORIX

We are committed to the health and well-being of our employees and offer a quality, competitive benefits package that provides valuable healthcare and financial protection for our workforce, while emphasizing a healthy work/life balance.

You Time

We want you to have the time needed to recharge and manage life with our competitive vacation policy, floating personal holidays, extensive leave options, our annual observed holidays, and more.

Family Care

Every family has a different journey and ORIX USA Group is proud to offer extensive benefit programs such as: infertility coverage, adoption support, child and elder care, fully paid maternity, and generous parental leave benefits to help you and your families along the way.

Flexible Work Arrangements

ORIX associates enjoy the advantages of our hybrid working model with a 3 day in-office minimum requirement.

ORIX USA is an equal opportunity employer, providing equal employment opportunities (EEO), and is committed to diversifying its workforce. All employees and applicants for employment will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, protected veteran status or other status protected by law. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment.

jobs.title: Senior Director, Asset Management
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary:

ORIX Growth Capital is seeking a Senior Director or Director for our San Francisco office to help continue successfully growing the ORIX Growth Capital platform, particularly in the Bay Area. This investment professional will have responsibility for new investments and investment performance including establishing and deepening relationships with venture capital and private equity funds, technology firms, and intermediaries, identifying and winning quality financings, leading diligence and investment approval, and assisting with ongoing portfolio success. ORIX Growth Capital is proud of its talented team, strong industry relationships, and commitment to long-term success.

ORIX Growth Capital is one of the leading growth lending platforms in the market. We provide flexible debt and equity capital to valuable, high-growth businesses, primarily in the technology and healthcare sectors in the U.S. and Canada.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Originate new investment opportunities that have a strong risk/return and are a fit for our investment strategy. Build and nurture a wide and deep network of origination relationships
  • Develop a complete understanding and the risk/return of an emerging growth company by evaluating the entity’s market opportunity, competition, product, sales and growth, operations, financial statements and projections, management strength, equity support, capital structure, and collateral
  • Extensive business development, transaction identification, analysis, and underwriting
  • Evaluation and recommendation must be synthesized in a clear and concise investment memo for investment decisions with investment committee
  • Monitor the financial performance and compliance of portfolio accounts, remain current on industry trends and competitive landscape, monitor the venture markets for relevant news, review legal documents, and collaborate with senior management in the growth and success of ORIX Growth Capital
  • Other projects and duties as needed
  • Some travel may be required.


Education, Skills and Experience:

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required; Master's degree preferred
  • 10+ years of venture and growth company, equity and debt investing, and/or investment banking expertise
  • Prior direct investing experience required; Lending/Credit or venture capital experience a plus
  • Wide range network in the venture and growth equity and growth company ecosystem
  • Knowledge of the venture and growth company sector with technology and/or healthcare sector expertise
  • Strong accounting/financial modeling and analytical skills supported by a high proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to comfortably work with multiple concurrent transactions
  • Sound business judgment and decision-making abilities
  • Excellent organizational, communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to plan, organize, and prioritize assignments, and to meet critical and established deadlines; Ability to manage multiple assignments simultaneously
  • Ability to learn quickly and demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a high-performance team
  • Flexibility and ability to work under pressure with tight deadlines and constant interruptions; Ability to adjust priorities in a rapidly changing environment

jobs.title: Associate, Investment Acquisitions
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary:

In July 2025, ORIX announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which ORIX USA would acquire a majority equity ownership of Hilco Global. This will be the foundation of a new asset-based investing arm, which will combine Hilco Global’s expertise in and access to tangible and intangible assets with ORIX USA’s capital and lending capabilities to capture this growing, compelling market opportunity. 

Hilco is a privately held diversified financial services company and the world’s preeminent authority on maximizing the value of assets. The firm delivers a unique blend of restructuring and principal investing solutions across the retail, commercial industrial, real estate, manufacturing, brand, and intellectual property sectors. For 38 years, Hilco Global has operated as a holding company of more than twenty specialized business units that act as an advisor, agent, or principal in diverse transactions. Hilco Global is based in Northbrook, Illinois and has more than 770 professionals operating on five continents.

Hilco Global has built a differentiated track record by harnessing the strength of its people and proprietary asset-level data to drive a true origination and execution advantage. Now, in partnership with ORIX, Hilco is launching a dedicated Asset Management platform to expand its investing capabilities. As part of this initiative, we are growing our investment team in both our Chicago and New York offices.

We are seeking a highly motivated and entrepreneurial Associate to play a key role in the execution of unique cross-capital structure, asset-based investment opportunities and serve as a founding team member of the newly launched Hilco Asset Management platform.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Drive supporting engine of asset-based and structured credit investments, including diligence, financial modeling, and documentation
  • Execute comprehensive credit underwriting across the capital structure, with a focus on complex, collateral-backed investments (e.g., ABL, FILO, DIP, IP-backed, Asset Acquisitions, etc)
  • Actively support the buildout of the investment platform, including origination strategy, internal infrastructure, and go-to-market positioning
  • Develop detailed investment memoranda for internal IC approval and external capital partners
  • Support the team with borrowers, intermediaries, and corporate / sponsor relationships, building long-term connectivity across the market
  • Collaborate with internal Hilco valuation, liquidation, and appraisal teams to leverage firmwide insights into asset value recovery and downside protection
  • Support ongoing portfolio monitoring and asset management including covenant compliance, amendment processes, and reporting
  • Track deal pipeline and work with the operations team to ensure deals are properly booked into internal position and risk reporting systems

Travel Requirements - 10%


Education, Skills and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, business or other related field
  • 2 – 4 years of experience in private credit, investment banking, asset-based lending (commercial banking), special situations, or restructuring


Required:

  • Strong foundational skills in financial modeling, credit analysis, and due diligence
  • Demonstrated experience supporting the execution of debt or structured credit transactions ideally with collateral-backed structures
  • Ability to draft and synthesize investment materials, including memos and internal presentations
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail, capable of managing multiple workstreams in a fast-paced environment
  • Effective communicator with a collaborative mindset and a desire to learn from senior team members
  • Interest in working in an entrepreneurial setting with exposure to complex deal structures and cross-capital solutions


Annual base salary gross: $135,000 - $155,000 (NY and IL only).  The base salary range represents the estimated low and high end of ORIX’s salary for this position based on geography. Actual base salaries will vary and will be based on various factors, such as candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and proficiency for the role. The base pay is one component of ORIX’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards and benefits include cash bonuses, long term incentives, health insurance, accident and life insurance, and retirement benefits.  Please visit our Benefits page for additional information.

Life at ORIX

We are committed to the health and well-being of our employees and offer a quality, competitive benefits package that provides valuable healthcare and financial protection for our workforce, while emphasizing a healthy work/life balance.

You Time

We want you to have the time needed to recharge and manage life with our competitive vacation policy, floating personal holidays, extensive leave options, our annual observed holidays, and more.

Family Care

Every family has a different journey and ORIX USA Group is proud to offer extensive benefit programs such as: infertility coverage, adoption support, child and elder care, fully paid maternity, and generous parental leave benefits to help you and your families along the way.

Flexible Work Arrangements

ORIX associates enjoy the advantages of our hybrid working model with a 3 day in-office minimum requirement.

jobs.title: Senior Director Debt Capital Markets
jobs.description:
Purpose and Job Summary

Lument provides comprehensive capital solutions for multifamily, affordable, seniors and health care real estate.  This includes a full suite of financing and strategic solutions such as investment banking, mortgage banking, private equity, a variety of direct balance sheet lending options, investment sales, and mergers and acquisitions.  The Debt Capital Markets (DCM) group is seeking a Senior Director responsible for developing strategic lending partners (SLP’s) focused heavily on life company lending through correspondent relationships, serving as the main point of contact for maintaining the relationships. This position will contribute to the growth of the business by leveraging unique life company lending products that will create valuable options to meet the lending needs of Lument borrowers focusing on all CRE asset classes. The Senior Director will be an integral part of the team as a player coach for the DCM Central Desk staff, responsible for a variety of areas related to SLP and life company products, marketing products internally to the origination platform, and serving as the product knowledge expert for both front end and back-end execution.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Serve as life company relationship and product manager
  • Leverage life company relationships and contacts to develop a stable of partners and correspondent relationships
  • Represent the firm to life company partners at industry events and through in person meetings
  • Perform review of life company loan submission packages for acceptability
  • Reviews lender submission packages from analysts/associates for deal placement to life companies
  • Reviews quotes and application packages from analyst/associates
  • Provides workflow oversight for life company deals in process and in servicing as needed
  • Manages Central Desk and directs associates/analysts in all facets of analysis and preparing deals for life company and other execution

Provides credible recommendations to supervisors and senior management based on market research and analysis

  • Monitors and assists originator and support staff throughout the due diligence process and closing process as needed
  • Demonstrate excellent client relations skills
  • Work professional and harmoniously with team and coworkers
  • Other projects and duties as assigned

Travel Requirements

15% - In person meetings with strategic lending partners and conferences as appropriate

Contacts

This position has frequent contact with all levels of employees and management. In addition, this role interacts with outside business partners, vendors, consultants, and other office visitors

Education, Skills & Experience Required

  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university required.
  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in the life company commercial mortgage lending industry.
  • Management or supervisory experience with a small team.
  • Strong proficiency with MS-Excel, word, Argus, and other computer programs needed.

Preferred

  • Product knowledge of CRE lending products across all asset classes.
  • Experienced with Bank, CMBS, Debt Fund, and Agency lending products.

jobs.title: Director, Infrastructure & Public Finance - Originations
jobs.description:
Job Summary:
ORIX Corporation USA’s Infrastructure & Public Finance group (IPF) is seeking an experienced originator to source high-yield private credit investments. The successful candidate will focus on securing land development opportunities for investment, primarily in Texas and the US Sunbelt states.

Key Responsibilities

  • Origination: Lead the end-to-end sourcing and closing of high-yield debt opportunities.

  • Execution: Leverage a deep understanding of risk underwriting and complex deal structuring to drive portfolio growth.

  • Networking: Utilize existing relationships with real estate developers to expand ORIX’s footprint in the residential and commercial sectors.

Candidate Qualifications

  • Proven Track Record: Demonstrated success in originating and closing high-yield private credit or real estate transactions.

  • Strategic Network: An established contact list of real estate developers.

  • Professional Background: Experience in Investment Banking, Private Credit, or Residential/Commercial Real Estate Development lending.

  • Risk Analysis:  Assist in the preparation and presentation of credit presentations for approval committees. Work as part of a team to develop independent and fact-based investment recommendations.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree from accredited four-year university in finance, economics, or related field required

  • Minimum 10 years of financial advisory, real estate development or investment banking experience

  • Master’s degree from accredited university preferred, CPA or CFA beneficial but not required.

About the IPF Team

The Infrastructure & Public Finance group is an innovative, flexible investor based in Dallas. The 14-person team provides senior and subordinate debt and preferred equity capital across a diverse range of public and private financings in a highly collaborative environment.

jobs.title: Infrastructure Equity, Senior Associate
jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. We recognize those who work diligently for us and reward them for personal and professional integrity, communication skills, distinct competencies and expertise in specific strategies, ability to collaborate as a team member and true dedication to the interests of our clients.

We thank you for your interest in joining the Barings team, and invite you to explore our current employment opportunities.

Job Title: Infrastructure Equity, Senior Associate

Corporate Title: Senior Associate

Business Unit: Global Infrastructure Equity 

Location: New York City

As part of Barings’ newly formed and expanding Global Infrastructure Equity group, the Senior Associate will play a key role supporting the full investment lifecycle across core, core‑plus, and value‑add infrastructure opportunities. This is a hands‑on role with responsibility spanning financial modeling, diligence, execution support, sector research, and ongoing portfolio management, offering meaningful exposure as the platform continues to grow.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead detailed financial modeling and support third-party diligence coordination.
  • Track emerging sector trends across power, transportation, energy, digital, and utility infrastructure
  • Prepare screening memos, valuation views, and market research.
  • Prepare Investment Committee materials and maintain data rooms.
  • Support transaction documentation and negotiation under senior guidance.
  • Assist with KPI dashboards, valuation files, and quarterly asset reporting.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally and assist with capital-raising materials.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree required, with Business or Finance preferred.
  • 3–5 years of relevant work experience in investment banking or private equity
  • Experience in analyzing financial statements and conducting due diligence
  • Advanced financial modeling skills
  • Familiarity with sectors such as energy transition, digital infra, transportation, and utility infrastructure
  • Project-finance modeling experience and infrastructure-specific metrics.
  • Strong IC writing and presentation skills.
  • Ability to prioritize and multi-task.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • CFA/CAIA progress a plus
  • Exposure to refinancings, bolt-ons, or asset-level KPIs.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills; interact with members of senior management, executives and line staff in a positive and collegial manner
  • Strong interpersonal, organizational, and collaboration skills
  • Ability to operate autonomously with transparency and integrity
  • Solutions focused mindset; creative

#LI-CM2

Base Salary Range: $120,000- $150,000 plus additional incentives

Requisite Skills

Acquisitions, Due Diligence, Financial Modeling, Private Equity

Additional Skills

Barings is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer; Minority/Female/Age/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/Individual with Disability/Protected Veteran. We welcome all persons to apply.

CORE BENEFITS & WELLNESS

  • Medical (including Virtual Care), Prescription, Dental, and Vision Coverage
  • Fitness Center Reimbursement Program (Including Online Memberships)

FINANCIAL WELL-BEING

  • Highly competitive 401(k) Plan with Company Match
  • Health Savings Account (HSA) with Company Contributions
  • Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) - Health Care & Dependent Care
  • Retirement Health Reimbursement Account

LIFE INSURANCE

  • Basic and Supplemental Life Insurance
  • Spouse and Child Life Insurance

TIME OFF, DISABILITY AND LEAVE OF ABSENCE

  • Paid Vacation, Sick Days and Annual Holidays
  • Paid Leave of Absences (Maternity Leave, Parental Leave, Caregiver Leave, Bereavement Time)
  • Short and Long Term Disability Plans

 OTHER BENEFITS

  • Education Assistance Program
  • Charitable Matching Gifts Program

jobs.title: Senior Asset Manager
jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. We recognize those who work diligently for us and reward them for personal and professional integrity, communication skills, distinct competencies and expertise in specific strategies, ability to collaborate as a team member and true dedication to the interests of our clients.

We thank you for your interest in joining the Barings team, and invite you to explore our current employment opportunities.

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Through active asset management and direct origination, we provide innovative solutions and access to differentiated opportunities across public and private capital markets. A subsidiary of MassMutual, Barings maintains a strong global presence with business and investment professionals located across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific.

Job Summary

The Senior Asset Manager will be based in the Melbourne Real Estate team and will report to the Senior Director, Major Projects. This position is an integral member of the Asset Management team, enthusiastically sharing their experience with others and assisting in the provision of a range of services to the business.  Working within a culture that thrives on teamwork, collaboration, performance outcomes and a passion for real estate, this senior manager will work to achieve optimum performance from a portfolio of commercial assets. The role will involve managing assets for Investors and Joint Venture partners. 

Primary Responsibilities

  • Lead and coordinate annually strategic asset planning process, focusing on each property’s key features and risks, performance, value add capital expenditure and customer strategy.
  • Informed by a strong understanding of the local and wider market conditions, competing assets, occupier business and an existing network of contacts in real estate, construction etc.
  • Demonstrate a proven track record in identifying value creation opportunities and executing strategies to realise such outcomes.
  • Demonstrate continuous improvement in process and compliance not limited to reporting, budgeting, valuation management, arrears management, WHS management, rent reviews, outgoings reconciliation and asset re-purposing.
  • Work closely with the Senior Director, Major Projects to review and negotiate significant leases and to resolve material issues where required.
  • Work with Property, Facilities Managers and other service providers in a way that they can enjoy clarity in understanding the asset goals, they experience inclusion and a sense of being an extension of our team, are inspired to ensure assets look and perform at their best.
  • Strengthen and enhance customer relationships to maximize customer satisfaction objectives and customer retention
  • In collaboration with the Barings ESG team, support and implement industry best practice sustainability initiatives across all aspects of the properties
  • Meet the legal and regulatory obligations of the Trustee and Manager of the respective Funds and Joint Venture agreements
  • Provide leadership, motivation and guidance to team members.
  • Comply with Barings procedures and probity requirements, anti-discrimination and Work Health and Safety legislation. Organise handover from Development Management team to Asset Management team and manage defects program.
  • Ensure effective communication lines are established and maintained with the Asset Team.
  • Demonstrate a collaborative and innovative approach and work with the Senior Director, Major Projects and team to deliver high quality project outcomes.
  • Meet project milestones for specific development projects, deliver project outcomes to a high professional standard, and demonstrate value for money and agreed financial returns to the business.

Qualifications

  • Qualification in property, finance or related field.
  • 15+ years' experience in an asset management role and a minimum of 7 years managing and/or leasing industrial assets in Melbourne.
  • Ability and track record to prepare strategic asset plans and clearly identify, articulate, and execute on those approved asset strategies.
  • Good market knowledge of commercial and industrial markets from an investor, leasing and capital transactions perspective.
  • Strong understanding of leasing process and valuation principles.
  • Knowledge of relevant Australian Acts, standards, codes and practices.
  • Ability to direct and lead outsourced property and facilities managers.
  • An understanding of customer experience, customer drivers and customer strategy execution.
  • A passion for ESG and implementing initiatives to enhance asset performance and occupier experience.
  • Proven customer engagement, dispute resolution and strategic relationship building skills.
  • Capability to understand and resolve complex property management, leasing and valuation issues.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to integrate quickly within a flexible team-oriented environment.
  • Proficiency with MS Office including Excel, Word, PowerPoint and budgeting tools.

#LI-GA1

Requisite Skills

Additional Skills

jobs.description:
At Barings, we are as invested in our associates as we are in our clients. Title: Real Estate Equity, Senior Associate

Corporate Title: Senior Associate

Department: U.S. Real Estate Equity

Location: Chicago, IL, Boston, MA, or Charlotte, NC

We are seeking a highly motivated Senior Associate to join our Real Estate Equity team, supporting acquisitions and asset management activities across the Eastern US. This individual will be deeply involved in analyzing new investment opportunities, maintaining and enhancing valuation models, preparing investment materials, and supporting execution from underwriting through closing.

This is an opportunity to join a well‑capitalized, high‑performing platform with meaningful exposure to senior leadership, investment committees, and cross‑functional real estate teams. The role offers hands‑on experience across acquisitions, valuation, leasing analysis, and portfolio performance.

Key Responsibilities

  • Underwrite and analyze potential acquisitions using complex ARGUS and Excel cash flow models.
  • Build, maintain, and refine valuation models for assigned assets, reflecting new leases, market changes, and updated assumptions.
  • Coordinate quarterly valuations with asset managers, regional directors, and third‑party appraisers; review appraisals and reconcile issues.
  • Monitor property performance through monthly reporting, hold/sell analyses, and variance reviews.
  • Evaluate economic, demographic, and market data to support acquisition decisions and portfolio strategy.
  • Prepare clear, concise investment committee briefs and supporting materials.
  • Analyze lease transactions, including net effective rents, and assess value impact.
  • Assist with annual budgets, business plans, quarterly portfolio updates, and ad hoc analyses.
  • Support the due diligence process, including legal, financial, and physical reviews for new acquisitions.
  • Provide support on acquisition financing, including lender coordination.
  • Maintain and update the acquisition pipeline in Salesforce.
  • Participate in recurring leasing calls and property‑level strategy discussions.
  • Develop a deep understanding of assigned assets, markets, and tenant drivers.
  • Engage with brokers, developers, and operators to evaluate opportunities and validate underwriting assumptions.

Qualifications

  • 3–5 years of experience in real estate investment analysis, asset valuation, or a related financial discipline.

Job Title: Real Estate Equity Acquisitions

Corporate Title: Associate Director

Location: Boston, MA

We are seeking a high‑impact Associate Director to join our Real Estate Acquisitions team and play a key role in evaluating and executing commercial real estate investments across the Eastern US. This individual will be a critical contributor to the full deal lifecycle—from initial underwriting through due diligence, structuring, and closing—while working closely with senior originators and investment committee leadership.

Job Title: CMBS Analyst

Corporate Title: Associate/Senior Associate

Department: Real Estate Debt

Location: New York City, NY

Barings’ Real Estate Debt platform is seeking a Senior Associate to join its CMBS team. This is a highly analytical role focused on underwriting, surveillance, and investment analysis across primary and secondary CMBS transactions. The Senior Associate will work closely with senior investment professionals on portfolio risk management, relative value analysis, and transaction execution. The role reports to the Head of CMBS.

Key Responsibilities

  • Perform loan-level and bond-level underwriting for new issue CMBS transactions, including cash flow analysis, market review, structural analysis, and downside scenario modeling.
  • Analyze collateral performance, credit enhancement, deal structure, and relative value across conduit and SASB transactions.
  • Conduct ongoing portfolio surveillance, including review of servicer reports, updated financials, leasing trends, watchlist assets, and secondary market activity.
  • Track CMBS issuance, spreads, trading activity, and performance trends to support portfolio management and investment decisions.
  • Support evaluation and execution of CMBS, SASB, and loan pool acquisitions, including transaction analytics and data review.
  • Build and maintain analytical tools and models to support underwriting, surveillance, and trading activities.
  • Leverage large datasets and contribute to technology-enabled and AI-driven solutions to enhance investment analysis and efficiency.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Business, or a quantitatively focused discipline required.
  • 2–5 years of relevant investment analysis experience; background in CMBS, structured credit, commercial real estate, or fixed income strongly preferred.
  • Strong fundamental credit skills with hands-on experience in cash flow modeling and structured product analysis.
  • Advanced proficiency in Excel and financial modeling; experience working with large, complex datasets.
  • Experience with Intex, Trepp, and/or Bloomberg preferred.
  • Ability and interest in developing technical skills such as Python or SQL.
  • Strong analytical, written, and verbal communication skills.
  • Detail-oriented, intellectually curious, and comfortable in a fast-paced, technically rigorous investment environment.

Job Title: Affordable Housing Investments Transaction Manager  

Corporate Title: Associate Director

Department: Real Estate - Affordable Housing Investments

Location: Chicago, Charlotte, Boston, or Los Angeles

Position Description

This position is responsible for working on transactions and with sponsors who specialize in the multifamily affordable and workforce housing sector.  The individual will be tasked to screen, analyze and manage the overall progress of loans originated by the platform.  After transactions go through initial prescreen and term sheet stages, the individual will work closely with the underwriting and closing teams to manage the progression of loans through internal committee approvals. The individual will also have an opportunity to originate loans directly while acting as transaction manager on other loans. Transactions will include debt investments in Low-Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC), Workforce (Middle-Income) and Mixed-Income housing properties.  This individual should have a good understanding of the multifamily affordable housing industry and should be able to navigate and build relationships with developers.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Drive the end‑to‑end execution of affordable and workforce (middle-income) multifamily debt transactions—owning the transaction from initial screen through term sheet, internal committee approvals, and closing
  • Serve as a primary point of contact to developers/sponsors, building durable relationships that advance the platform’s production goals in LIHTC, workforce, and mixed‑income properties
  • Screen & size new opportunities against firm credit parameters; assess feasibility and recommend go/no‑go with clear rationale
  • Manage deal execution—in collaboration with the underwriting team, track diligence, surface issues early, drive resolutions with internal partners (underwriting, legal/closing, servicing, asset management) and external parties
  • Prepare and present clear, data‑driven materials for internal committee reviews; translate feedback into actionable next steps to maintain velocity to close

Requirements

  • Minimum of five (5) years of experience in affordable housing lending
  • Bachelor's Degree in Real Estate or Finance
  • Experience in loan originations and transacting in affordable housing (AH) loans
  • Knowledge of LIHTC programs and how they work
  • Ability to understand different loan structures within the multifamily space; Knowledge of sizing and analyzing AH loans
  • Brings experience in loan structuring and underwriting balance sheet loan products
  • Experience interacting internally - with platform underwriters, analysts, servicing and asset management – and externally on transaction matters
  • Ability to lead the transaction process with borrowers and sponsors
  • Solid understanding of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Ability to model or revise financials models, as needed

Skills and Competencies

  • Strong written and verbal communications skills
  • Strong interpersonal skills to develop and/or cultivate relationships
  • Can be strategic in winning opportunities
  • Ability to prioritize multiple competing tasks
  • Detailed attention to presentation and strong organizational skills
  • Can communicate effectively and able to present loan transactions to investment committee
  • Results oriented in a deadline driven work environment
  • High energy, self-motivated and ability to make immediate, logical decisions
  • Brings transparency and a “team approach” mentality

Job Title: Affordable Housing Investments - Senior Asset Manager   

Corporate Title: Director

Department: Real Estate - Affordable Housing Investments

Location: Boston, Charlotte, Los Angeles or Chicago

Position Description
 

The individual will be tasked to lead asset management responsibilities for primarily multifamily properties, both debt and equity.  This includes working closely with other asset managers to closely monitor and report periodically on the overall debt and equity portfolio. This individual will also work closely with the underwriting team to assist with capital calls when transactions are ready to fund and manage the assets after funding. Working closely with internal and external partners to identify issues early on and work with their manager to propose viable solutions to address identified issues, when necessary. The position will require consistency of approach to solutions and managing the portfolio in a manner that minimizes negative impacts or losses. 

 Primary Responsibilities:

  • Lead asset management for a diverse national portfolio of multifamily properties, ensuring optimal performance and compliance
  • Asset manage LIHTC debt and equity transactions, applying deep understanding of program requirements and deal structures
  • Monitor and analyze assets across debt and equity portfolios, driving informed decision-making
  • Partner with loan servicing teams to collect and analyze property operating data
  • Generate annual risk ratings and other portfolio performance summary reports
  • Build strong relationships with external partners, developers, and operators, proactively requesting and evaluating property data to support strategic objectives
  • Support capital call processes, ensuring timely and accurate execution of funding requirements
  • Present to investment committee to communicate deal progress, surface critical issues, and support informed credit decisions
  • Occasional travel may be required

Requirements:

  • 8-10 years of commercial real estate debt experience in multifamily
  • Bachelor's Degree in Real Estate or Finance
  • Experience with both LIHTC debt and equity
  • Knowledge of affordable and workforce housing industry including LIHTC is helpful but not required
  • Solid understanding of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Experience in portfolio construction lending is a plus  

Skills and Competencies:

  • Proactive self‑starter with outstanding communication, analytical, and organizational strengths, adept at managing multiple priorities.
  • Excels in team‑based environments while confidently driving work forward independently in a dynamic setting.
  • Ability to build cohesive relationships that incorporates core values, builds trust and promotes a productive environment among teammates and within the overall Barings Real Estate platform
  • Strong desire to learn and work collaboratively to help grow the Affordable Housing platform

Title: Associate, European Private Finance (French speaking)

Reports to: Senior Director, Global Private Finance

Location: London (with travel to France and other European geographies as needed)

Team Overview

Barings Private Credit & Global Private Finance Division

Barings is a leading global asset management firm with over $481 billion AUM. Barings Private Credit includes middle-market corporate credit, private placement debt and infrastructure debt. With more than 50 years of credit experience, the team understands how to effectively invest in private debt instruments to evaluate and capture the additional yield potential of illiquid and less liquid credit-based investments.

Barings Private Finance is a Private Credit sub-team of over 100 dedicated investment professionals located in North America, Europe and Asia Pacific focused on providing investors access to direct lending opportunities up and down the capital structure in middle-market companies.

The team sources global direct lending investment opportunities across a spectrum of risk/return profiles:: senior secured loans, second lien, unitranche, mezzanine debt, equity co-investments. Investment professionals directly originate and underwrite every deal they invest in and have the ability to create highly customised portfolios to meet investor needs.

Barings Private Finance in Europe & France

Barings has substantially grown its European private credit strategy. The European Private Finance team counts 40+ investment professionals in London covering the European markets. France is one of the key investment geographies.

Job Summary

With the current commitment to France and to strengthen the existing investment team in London, Barings is seeking to recruit an Associate to assist in providing cash flow based financing solutions to private equity sponsored companies across a variety of industries. The position is located in our London office.

Role & Responsibilities

  • Analysing new investment opportunities and typically evaluate companies, the markets in which they operate, and the risks related to arranging and investing in private debt securities

  • Assisting in preparation of credit papers and supporting the deal team’s presentation of a transaction to Investment Committee

  • Financial modelling of transactions including conducting appropriate sensitivity analysis

  • Manage and conduct performance analysis with monitoring responsibilities over certain portfolio companies in coordination with other team members

  • Participate in interactions with management teams, private equity sponsors, origination sources and other investment professionals

  • Support broader deal team around managing flow info with advisors/intermediaries and supporting certain elements of bid letters, term sheets and definite documents

  • Over time, work alongside the team to further develop the investment strategy in France, grow an effective local network (sponsors, key advisors and intermediaries) to identify new investment ideas.

Required Experience & Qualifications

  • Fluent French Speaker

  • 2+ years of corporate finance transaction experience in M&A, debt financing, leveraged finance or private debt

  • Undergraduate degree preferably with a focus on Finance

  • Experience underwriting, structuring and executing relevant transactions for financial sponsors and/or corporate clients

  • Solid technical skills and capable of assessing deal opportunities and participating in the transaction execution process

  • Rigorous, fast and reliable in execution with keen attention to detail

  • An investor mindset: open, mobile and mature in risk management

  • Passionate about investment – strong development potential and ambition

  • Good interpersonal skills a team player mindset

  • Multicultural, adaptable and a resilient and autonomous mind

Requisite Skills

Direct Lending, French Language, Private Credit, Private Financing

Additional Skills

Barings is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer; Minority/Female/Age/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity/Individual with Disability/Protected Veteran. We welcome all persons to apply.

Position Title: Investment Grade Credit Analyst

Business Title: Director

Job Summary

Barings’ Investment Grade Corporate team is seeking an investment professional with Five or more years of work experience for a senior credit analyst position.  The candidate selected will join a global Investment Grade Corporate Credit team, including a seven-person analyst team, in London.   The individual will be responsible for analyzing and evaluating investment opportunities for the Investment Grade Corporates team, which manages assets in excess of $80 billion.   The Director level candidate will have the responsibility for lead coverage on Developed Market Banks within the Financials sector.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Provide analysis of investment grade corporate credits within the Financials (Banks) sector
  • Financial analysis and modeling

  • Meet with management teams, develop industry contacts, and utilize other information sources to become an expert in assigned coverage
  • Produce clear and concise research reports based on independent observation and analysis
  • Produce quarterly industry reviews and communicate recommendations to portfolio management
  • Collaborate with other Barings’ investment teams optimizing efficiencies and framing relative value within sectors

  • CFA and/or MBA preferred
  • Client presentation experience preferred

There is an Analyst level position available for this team. This individual will be responsible for the maintenance, management and use of the private investment platforms. The ideal candidates will have knowledge of accounting platforms within our industry along with a broad accounting expertise.  Our teams consist of highly skilled agile individuals who are expected to provide accurate, timely, and efficient services.  This individual will be responsible for the following areas:

  • Private platforms subject matter expert

Barings is a leading global financial services firm dedicated to meeting the evolving investment and capital needs of our clients and customers. Job Summary

We are looking for a Director, Client Portfolio Management to join in our Hong Kong office. Acting as subject matter expert, this person collaborates closely with investment teams, sales teams, and other internal stakeholders to advance business development, client servicing, and product development for targeted investment strategies. This position is dedicated to supporting ongoing expansion and client engagement within the APAC region, across Barings’ fixed income strategies. The successful candidate will contribute to the firm’s substantial global public fixed income offerings—including high yield, investment grade, loans, emerging market debt, structured credit, and multi-strategy credit.

Title: Investment Grade Credit, Senior Analyst (Utilities or Financials)
Level: Associate Director or Director

Business Unit: Investment Grade Corporate

Location: Charlotte, NC or Boston, MA

Job Summary

Barings’ Investment Grade Corporate team is seeking an experienced investment professional to join the platform as a senior credit analyst. The team manages a large, diversified investment grade corporate credit portfolio and is responsible for fundamental credit research, relative value analysis, and investment recommendations across the capital structure.

This role will lead coverage responsibilities, with primary ownership of either the Utilities or Financials sector. The successful candidate will conduct in-depth fundamental analysis, partner closely with portfolio managers on security selection and risk management, and serve as a key contributor to sector strategy and investment decisions.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Provide analysis of investment grade corporate credits within the Financials or Utilities sector
  • Financial analysis and modeling
  • Evaluate companies and the risks related to investing in debt securities across the capital structure
  • Individual security analysis, including capital structure and indenture analysis, cash flow and liquidity analysis and total return and relative value assessment
  • Meet with management teams, develop industry contacts, and utilize other information sources to become an expert in assigned coverage
  • Produce clear and concise research reports based on independent observation and analysis
  • Produce quarterly industry reviews and communicate recommendations to portfolio management
  • Collaborate with other Barings’ investment teams optimizing efficiencies and framing relative value within sectors
  • Assist with special ad-hoc projects, presentations and initiatives

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of credit analyst experience—either investment grade or high yield/bank debt/privates
  • CFA and/or MBA preferred
  • Client presentation experience preferred
  • Excellent writing and communications skills
  • Ability to work as a member of a fast-paced, transaction oriented team
  • Self-starter, must be organized and able to work independently
  • Ability to prioritize work and multi-task responsibilities
  • Ability to perform under pressure and meet time-sensitive deadlines
  • Strong computer skills, specifically in Microsoft Office applications, Bloomberg, etc
  • Some travel required

Base Salary Range: $140,000-190,000 and additional incentive compensation.

Job Title: Senior Associate – Real Estate Debt Asset Management

Primary Responsibilities

  • Serve as a key point of coordination across servicing (in-house and third-party), portfolio management, legal, and originations teams to support loan-level decisions.
  • Support the annual loan review process for the Core Portfolio, including:
    • Reviewing third-party spreads of property-level operating statements
    • Analyzing updated sponsor financial statements
    • Updating collateral valuations using DCF analysis in Argus
    • Assigning internal loan quality ratings using DSCR, LTV, Debt Yield, and other metrics
  • Analyze lease transactions, including net effective rent calculations and the impact on value.

  • Assist with monitoring, modification requests, and enforcement actions.
  • Maintain watchlist oversight using covenant testing; present findings quarterly.
  • Join marketing and leasing calls; evaluate and model leasing economics and valuation impacts.
  • Prepare quarterly performance write-ups and risk ratings for High Yield loans; coordinate with third-party providers on valuation updates.
  • Contribute to ESG-related initiatives across the managed portfolio.

Requirements

  • 3–5 years of experience in commercial real estate investment analysis, debt asset management, or valuation.
  • Bachelor’s degree required, ideally in business, finance, real estate, or a related field.
  • Advanced proficiency in Argus and Microsoft Excel.
  • Strong understanding of finance and real estate investment concepts.
  • Exceptional quantitative, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within cross-functional teams.

jobs.title: Finance Manager - Tech Opportunities
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW:

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. 

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

TECH OPPORTUNITIES OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Tech Opportunities (https://www.baincapitaltechopportunities.com/) is an affiliate of Bain Capital established to invest in growing technology businesses. We partner with exceptional entrepreneurs and management teams to help them succeed through critical phases of growth and expansion. We provide flexible capital, strategic and operational resources, and deep industry expertise backed by the global reach of the Bain Capital platform. Tech Opps focuses on large and growing end markets: application software, infrastructure and cybersecurity, healthcare IT, and fintech and payments. The fund targets companies with innovative or disruptive technology and supports their transformational growth.

Investments are deployed globally and across a broad array of archetypes, including mid-market control buyouts, late-stage growth capital, tactical opportunities, and cross-platform investments alongside other parts of Bain Capital. 

OPPORTUNITY

We’re seeking a Finance Manager to lead funds accounting oversight, investor reporting and operational finance for Bain Capital Tech Opportunities. You will review and validate financial statements and reporting produced by our third-party fund administrator, own audit coordination, and partner with deal teams and Investor Relations to deliver accurate, timely financial reporting and strong controls. Success in the first 6–12 months includes consistent, timely financial reporting with clear reconciliations, a smooth year-end audit, and delivery reporting automation or process improvement projects.

POSITION OVERVIEW

Great opportunity with room to grow career wise in a fast paced/high achieving fund at Bain Capital. Will have exposure to and collaborate on all aspects of finance/operational functions of Bain Tech Opportunities funds (currently 3 funds). Primarily responsible for the oversight, preparation, review, and participation in the following activities for Tech Opps Business Unit:

  • Review of quarterly NAVs and capital reporting to investors.

  • Review quarterly financial statements, management fee and carry calculations, as well as other deliverables for investor reporting.

  • Manage year-end audit process with outside accountants and facilitate related requests.

  • Manage third party fund administrator on all aspects of reporting/operations.

  • Oversight of cash availability and fund flows.

  • Work with deal staff and CFO to assist in the preparation of quarterly portfolio company valuations & prepare summarized data for the Valuation Committee.

  • Respond to requests and inquiries of limited partners and their representatives.

  • Review and analyze portfolio company financials in preparation for quarterly portfolio review meetings

  • Work with Investor Relations in preparation of presentation materials, information posted to investor website, DD requests and fundraising documents.

  • Analysis of various fund activities to ensure compliance with partnership agreements.

  • Work with the Finance team and CFO on projects to enhance the product of the team, department and firm.

  • Other ad hoc responsibilities.

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 6+ years’ experience at an Investment Advisor and/or public accounting role preferred. Private and public exposure a plus.

  • Excellent project management and organizational skills; ability to effectively prioritize multiple projects with the highest attention to detail.

  • Creative problem-solving ability and sound judgment.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with all levels.

  • Team player.

  • Comfort working across a variety of technology platforms, including CRMs, HR and finance systems (e.g., Workday), AI-powered tools, and other enterprise software; demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technologies.

  • Experience with or curiosity about AI-powered tools for financial analysis, reporting automation, and workflow optimization; demonstrated ability to leverage technology to drive process efficiencies.

  • Strong data literacy, including the ability to work with large datasets and familiarity with data visualization or business intelligence tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar).

EDUCATION

  • Four-year college degree with a strong academic background.

  • Major in accounting, finance or related degree required preferred.

  • CPA, CFA or equivalent qualification a plus.

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $125,000 – 145,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

jobs.title: PM Associate, Private Credit
jobs.description:
PM Associate, Private Credit

Established in 1984, Bain Capital is one of the world's leading private investment firms with approximately $105 billion in assets under management. Bain Capital manages a range of strategies including private equity, public equity, credit, venture capital, real estate, life sciences, and impact investments.  Our competitive advantage is grounded in a diligence-intensive, value-added investment approach that enables the firm to deliver industry-leading returns for its investors. Headquartered in Boston, Bain Capital has offices in Chicago, Dublin, Guangzhou, Hong Kong, London, Madrid, Melbourne, Mumbai, Munich, New York, Palo Alto, San Francisco, Seoul, Shanghai, Sydney, and Tokyo.

BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit is a leading global credit specialist with approximately $53 billion in assets under management.  We invest across the full spectrum of credit strategies, including asset-based finance, direct lending, leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, non-performing loans, real estate and other hard assets and equities.  Through a wide diversity of mandates, we have the ability to invest in every level of a company or asset’s capital structure from secured debt to equity and can provide holistic solutions to growing companies with unique financing needs.

Our investment process is characterized by detailed business and financial analysis and a culture of vigorous debate.  We combine a consulting-based approach to industry, competitor, and business analysis with an in-depth examination of a target company’s financial performance and capital structure.  Our goal is to select the best industries, the best companies, and the best relative value among the many securities each company offers.

Bain Capital Credit has a world-class team of over 110 investment professionals with extensive experience analyzing and managing high-yield investments. 

PRIVATE CREDIT GROUP

Bain Capital Credit’s Private Credit Group evaluates investment opportunities across the spectrum in the middle market, or typically companies with $10-150M of EBITDA located in North America, Europe and Asia Pacific.  With a dedicated global team of over 40 investment professionals, we can lead investments across the capital structure in senior secured first lien, second lien and unitranche loans, subordinated debt, and preferred or common equity co-investments. Our dedicated global team affords us the ability to diligence the most complex situations and provides private capital to those companies. 

PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT TEAM

The Portfolio Management Team works directly with senior management on group strategy and fund positioning, with a focus on asset allocation, return optimization, risk management, liability management and fund analysis. The Private Credit Group has $21 billion AUM today across two business development companies, Bain Capital Specialty Finance, Inc. (NYSE: BCSF) and Bain Capital Private Credit (BCPC), the flagship Global Direct Lending and Middle Market Credit Partners funds, as well as several other separately managed accounts, Private Credit CLOs and structured fund products. We are looking to hire an Associate.

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE

  • Analyze current portfolio positioning and capitalization across funds and managed accounts in the firm’s Private Credit strategies

  • Support in evaluating, structuring, and executing on funds, structured funds (rated notes, collateralized fund obligations), financing structures (SPVs, CLOs) and other structured products

  • Contribute to the assessment and execution of strategic growth planning initiatives within the Private Credit Group

  • Build and maintain complex financial models of the Private Credit Group’s funds and managed accounts

  • Generate the analytical framework used by the portfolio management team, investors, and other related parties

  • Conduct a wide range of research and analysis for use in portfolio management, investor requests and internal analytics

  • Communicate with senior management and investors on current portfolio construction and return information

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials and a demonstrated track record of professional high performance

  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience with a strong preference for prior credit market experience including leveraged lending, private credit or structured credit experience

  • Excellent communication skills; able to synthesize complicated analyses concisely and articulate conclusions in a compelling manner.  Open to spirited debate over ideas.

  • Strong financial statement analysis and modeling abilities. Strong understanding of public and private capital markets, corporate securities and capital structures

  • High agility and an ability to multi-task and work across the organization on a wide range of workstreams and deliverables.

  • Experience with middle market direct lending, or CLO or other structured / financing products is a plus

Compensation:
Expected Annual Base Salary $140,000-150,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

Bain Capital is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.

jobs.title: Senior Finance Associate - CLO
jobs.description:
BAIN CAPITAL CREDIT OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Credit is a leading global credit specialist with $61 billion in assets under management. We invest across the credit spectrum and in credit-related strategies, including leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, structured products, private middle market loans, bespoke capital solutions, distressed securities and assets, non-performing loans, hard assets, and equities. Since inception, we have made a cultural commitment to investor alignment. We are a private partnership and invest a significant amount of our own capital alongside our limited partners. We have approximately $1 billion of employee co-investment committed across Bain Capital Credit funds as of September 30, 2025. Bain Capital Credit’s investment professionals are dedicated to sourcing, analyzing, documenting, and executing attractive credit opportunities across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific and are supported by robust operational and technical resources.

BAIN CAPITAL OVERVIEW

With approximately $215 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,985+ employees in 24 offices on four continents. 

We partner differently to help people and companies embrace possibility and realize potential. Founded as a private partnership in 1984, we have fostered a culture of innovation, entrepreneurialism, and agility, empowering our people to define and own their career trajectories. Today, our partnership approach enables us to pursue strategic growth, build enduring relationships with a robust external network, and collaborate across our integrated platform to connect the deep and diverse expertise that unlocks breakthrough insights.

Our people are the heart of our advantage. Colleagues at all levels have a seat at the table as they tackle business challenges with a principal investor mindset. By asking incisive questions, respectfully challenging one another, and remaining intellectually agile, we work together to achieve exceptional outcomes. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Will include some or all of the following for Collateralized Loan Obligations (CLO) and other fixed income funds:

  • Oversight of accounting and financial statement preparation for three funds investing CLOs

    • Quarterly financial statements and year end audit

    • Monthly estimates

    • Weekly internal reporting

  • Interaction with tax accountants for the preparation of tax returns and K-1’s / PFICs.

  • Oversight of all aspects of CLO Indenture compliance and financial reporting including:

    • Daily analysis of investment activity and compliance reporting.

    • Monthly review of reconciliation with Trustee’s Indenture compliance reports.

    • Review of funds’ quarterly distributions.

    • Weekly performance reporting.

    • Assist in preparation of investor reports and requests.

    • Intercompany reporting (e.g. Investor Relations, Diligence, Bain Corporate, G.P., Coinvest, Compliance, Tax, Legal, and other Bain Capital investment divisions)

    • Frequent / Challenging / Unique Ad hoc requests including trade recommendations for portfolio manager

    • Analysis of funds’ performance

    • Application of provisions described in complex legal documents (e.g. Indentures) including closing and modeling new funds.

  • Oversight of all aspects of Credit Agreement compliance and reporting for various CLO warehouses and other credit facilities, which includes items listed in point above as well as:

  • Borrowing base management and availability, ensuring compliance and timely funding of new investments.

  • Stay abreast on the current pipeline of deals to anticipate cash needs and ensure best optimization.

  • Oversight of all credit agreement financial covenants and compliance certificate reporting.

  • Close interaction with Portfolio Management team to address issues surrounding investment objectives and constraints within each fund’s investment mandate. Responsible for having an understanding of asset classes owned within each fund, which may include bank loans, bonds, equities, credit default swaps, structured products and FX hedges.

  • Close interaction with deal staff to address any question that arises as part of the reporting process.  Responsible for understanding the nature of their question and addressing accordingly either through providing additional information as needed or developing new solutions to address their needs.

  • Close interaction with Operations and outside Administrators when issues are discovered as part of the reporting process.  Responsible for determining the root cause of the issues encountered, determining effective solutions, and then overseeing the implementation of those solutions. 

  • Close interaction with Investor Relations to address any questions from investors.

  • Close interaction with internal IT and outside consultants to achieve implementation of systematic solutions/efficiencies in relation to compliance and accounting systems and recurring reporting requests.  Assist in the design of solutions, as well as ownership of the user acceptance testing and implementation of solutions.  Propose new initiatives as challenges/inefficiencies arise.

GENERAL QUALIFICATIONS

  • 3+ years of work experience in a rigorous environment. Prior experience with CLOs or other structured products a plus.

  • Audit or tax experience in public accounting a plus.

  • Strong ability to prioritize multiple responsibilities and work under tight deadlines.

  • Self-starter with initiative. Ability to work unsupervised.

  • Strong analytical problem-solving skills.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with capacity to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm.

  • Ability to decipher complex legal documents.

  • Dedicated team player with a strong sense of ownership and follow-through skills

  • Detail-oriented, conscientious and energetic professional.

  • Strong Excel skills including the ability to develop efficient Excel templates from scratch.  Experience with macros not needed.

  • Strong ability to think outside the box to develop solutions to Frequent / Challenging / Unique Ad hoc requests in order to deliver the desired result/information when quite often starting from a blank slate.

REQUIRED EDUCATION

  • Four-year college degree with a strong academic background.

  • Major in accounting, finance and/or related degree preferred.

  • CPA or equivalent qualification a plus.  Work toward or an interest in pursuing a CFA charter a plus. 

Compensation:

Expected Annual Base Salary $90,000 – 105,000

Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications and geographic location. In addition to a competitive base salary, this position may be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based upon factors such as individual impact, team and firm performance. Bain Capital offers a competitive benefits package designed to support employees’ health, financial security, family needs, and overall well-being.

jobs.title: Aviation Asset Management Associate
jobs.description:
With approximately $205 billion of assets under management, Bain Capital is one of the world’s leading private investment firms. We create lasting impact for our investors, teams, businesses, and the communities in which we live. Over four decades we have strategically grown our platform to focus on Private Equity, Growth & Venture, Capital Solutions, Credit & Capital Markets, and Real Assets. Today, our team includes 1,950+ employees in 24 offices on four continents.

BAIN CAPITAL SPECIAL SITUATIONS OVERVIEW

Bain Capital Special Situations has $24 billion in assets under management and has invested $19 billion in ~250 deals across a balanced geographic mix of investments in North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific since its founding in 2018. We provide bespoke capital solutions to meet the diverse needs of companies, entrepreneurs, and asset owners - in all market cycles. The strategy brings together equity and credit expertise, as well as corporate and real asset expertise, to provide solutions which cannot be met by traditional providers or others funds within Bain Capital. Our dedicated, global team of approximately 145 investment and portfolio professionals contribute the local expertise and capabilities that enable these diverse investments.

Aviation is one of our key focus areas, both today and through the market cycle. Over our history, we have invested close to $5 billion in Special Situations aviation investments. Acquiring physical aircraft, including commercial, cargo, and private jets, has been a particular focus with over $2 billion invested in this sub-sector over the past few years. Going forward, we expect to build off our industry-leading track record to continue to grow our presence in aircraft hard assets, including investing dedicated capital for this investment strategy.  

PORTFOLIO GROUP OVERVIEW

Bain Capital has a dedicated global Portfolio Group within the firm’s Special Situations business. The Portfolio Group is responsible for fund and asset management and leads the strategy and analysis on fund construction, risk, and opportunities for the Global Special Situations business.

The Portfolio Group works side-by-side with Special Situations leadership and deal teams and provides extensive support, whenever and wherever needed on analytical, strategic, and operating issues. These capabilities have helped many Bain Capital companies develop and execute strategies to move swiftly to gain competitive advantage and achieve superior growth. We currently own ~$7 billion of aircraft assets across several platforms. Given this scale and complexity, the Portfolio Group is a highly valued and fully integrated piece of our aircraft strategy. We are currently hiring an Associate to work as part of the Portfolio Group focused on aircraft hard assets in our New York City office.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide analytical support to inform investment decisions, including new asset acquisitions, financings, sales, etc.
  • Act as a thought partner to the deal team, leveraging modeling, data, and analytics
  • Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders around all key asset management actions
  • Work on all aspects of the portfolio, including new deals, ongoing portfolio work, and ultimately value realization
  • Focus on modeling, data analytics, industry research, and internal/external reporting, all with the goal of optimizing investment outcomes
  • Develop analytical tools and maintain systems to assist in strategy setting, returns optimization, and capital formation
  • Attend board meetings, global industry events, etc.

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Strong academic credentials (BS/BS in finance, economics, accounting and/or related degree preferred)
  • 2-4 years of data analytics, risk analytics, portfolio management, or strategic finance experience; prior background in aviation or aircraft leasing is a strong plus but not required
  • High proficiency in Excel; ability to build and iterate analytical tools and dashboard with experience through former roles, internships or coursework in managing datasets and running financial models / scenario analytics
  • Ability to synthesize large, ambiguous data sets into clear insights
  • High proficiency in Powerpoint with ability to effectively communicate insights from analyses to diverse stakeholders
  • Strong interpersonal skills with ability to interact with outside parties and personnel across all levels of the firm
  • Strong oral and written communication skills with ability to distill analysis into crisp recommendations

Compensation

Expected Base Salary Range: $125,000 – $150,000

This is the expected base salary range for this position. Actual base salary will be determined by a wide range of factors including but not limited to role, function, level, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.
