jobs.title: Vice President - Originations
jobs.description:
We at Bridge acknowledge and appreciate the uniqueness of each individual, understanding that our people are the key to our success. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels respected and valued. Our dedication extends beyond our business goals to making a positive impact in our communities and upholding Sustainability and Responsibility (S&R) principles at our properties and corporate locations. We evaluate all candidates based on merit, without any discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by law. As an equal opportunity employer, we are devoted to creating an inclusive hiring process. We actively work to eliminate barriers and provide reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities. Our goal is to provide fair opportunities and access throughout the recruitment, hiring, and employment experience. Your specific needs are important to us. If you need accommodations related to a disability or any other protected status during the recruitment process, please inform us.

Make an impact

Bridge Debt Strategies is seeking a motivated VP of Originations to support the sourcing, underwriting, and execution of direct lending investments. This individual will work closely with senior leaders to evaluate opportunities, manage transactions from initial review through closing, and support portfolio performance throughout the life of each investment.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Focus on sourcing, underwriting, structuring, and closing real estate debt investments, working closely with senior originators and Investment Management Committee members
  • Develop and maintain strong relationships with sponsors/borrowers, mortgage brokers, and other intermediaries in order to source new direct lending opportunities
  • Travel to borrower meetings, property tours, and industry events to support deal sourcing and execution
  • Underwrite potential direct lending opportunities by modeling and reviewing detailed financial models, including cash flow projections, value creation, lease-up, and refinance or sale exit scenarios
  • Analyze credit risk, sponsorship strength, market fundamentals, and collateral quality for each investment
  • Coordinate the originations, transaction management and deal teams through underwriting, investment management committee approval, and closing
  • Lead the preparation of loan investment summaries and internal credit memos for presentation to the Investment Management Committee
  • Act as a resource to the asset management team post-closing, providing underwriting insight and support on amendments, extensions, and ongoing credit matters

What you should bring

  • 3 to 8 years of experience in real estate lending/originations, debt underwriting, investment banking and/or capital markets. Title is TBD based on experience.
  • Bachelor’s degree from a top-tier university
  • Strong credit, structuring and underwriting experience across all commercial real estate property types, with a focus on transitional assets
  • Ability to independently lead underwriting, structuring, and execution of real estate debt investments
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong focus on deal execution and relationship building
  • Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills
  • Ability to manage multiple transactions simultaneously and meet aggressive timelines
  • Proven ability to work independently while collaborating effectively in a team-oriented environment

What you can be a part of 

As a member of our Debt Strategies team, you’ll be part of a dynamic, multifaceted group at the center of an expanding platform. This role offers high visibility across the organization, with meaningful exposure to senior leadership and opportunities to collaborate closely with investment, capital markets, and portfolio management teams. You’ll contribute to strategic decision-making, gain hands-on experience across the lifecycle of our investments, and position yourself for strong upward mobility within a team that values performance, partnership, and professional growth.

Compensation Details

The anticipated wage scale for candidates who will work in New York is $250,000-340,000 per year. The hired applicant will also be eligible for an annual performance based discretionary bonus. The final pay offered to a successful candidate will be dependent on several factors that may include but are not limited to the type and years of experience within the job, the type of years and experience within the industry, education, etc. Bridge Investment Group is a multi-state employer and this pay scale may not reflect positions that work in other states or locations

What we offer

  • Full Insurance benefit suite including Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, Vision Insurance, Critical Illness Insurance, Accident Insurance, Short Term Disability, Legal & Identity Theft Insurance, and Pet Insurance.
  • Company paid Life Insurance (option to buy additional available) and Long-Term Disability.
  • Access to benefits concierge service.
  • Access to Mental Health & Well-Being service.
  • Commuter Benefits: Bridge participates in the NY Commuter Program
  • 401K: Bridge Investment Group will match your contributions dollar-for-dollar, up to 6% of your pay. These contributions are fully vested immediately. Eligible employees are automatically enrolled at a 4% contribution rate. *The employee must be at least 21 years of age and have worked for the Company for at least 60 days.
  • Paid Time Off: Employee will accrue 5.23 hours of paid time off per pay period for a total of 17 days per year.
  • 11 Paid Holidays per year.
  • Following six (6) months of employment at the Company, you will be eligible per birth, adoption or placement of a child for four (4) weeks of paid parental leave as the primary caregiver to the child or two weeks of paid parental leave as the secondary caregiver to the child. Following two years of employment at the Company, you will be eligible for twelve weeks of paid parental leave per birth, adoption, or placement of a child if you are the primary caregiver of the child.
  • Tuition Reimbursement: Up to $5,000 per year of pre-approved tuition expenses will be reimbursed upon submission of approved documentation. Repayment obligations may apply if employment terminates prior to 24 months.

jobs.title: Vice President - Fund Analytics
jobs.location: Salt Lake City Office, Sandy, UT
jobs.description:
Make an impact

Bridge Fund Financial Services is seeking a Vice President of Fund Analytics to play a critical role in shaping and delivering analytical insights across our private equity platform. This individual will own fund-level analytics, performance measurement, and reporting, partnering closely with Investment, Finance, Investor Relations, and senior leadership to support decision-making, fund management, and investor communications. The role blends deep analytical rigor with strong business judgment, requiring the ability to translate complex data into clear insights for internal and external stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead fund-level performance analytics across investment vehicles, including ownership of IRR, MOIC, DPI, TVPI, and attribution analysis

  • Analyze historical and projected fund performance to inform investment strategy and capital allocation decisions

  • Build andmaintainfinancial dashboards and reporting tools in Power BI and Excel (Pivot Tables, Power Query, DAX)

  • Support quarterly reporting, investor presentations, fundraising, and due diligence in partnership with Investor Relations

  • Ensure accuracy, consistency, and clarity of analytics across internal and external materials

  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Investment, Finance, Fund Accounting, and Operations teams tomaintaindata integrity and alignment

  • Support valuation, forecasting, and fund-level planning processes

  • Enhance and scale analytics frameworks, reporting infrastructure, and data workflows across systems

  • Leverage technology and automation to improve efficiency, transparency, and analytical depth

  • Mentor junior team members and contribute to best practices and knowledge sharing across the firm

What you should bring

  • 6–10 years of experience in private equity, asset management, alternatives, investment analytics, fund operations, or related financial roles

  • Strong understanding of private equity fund structures, performance metrics, and cash flow dynamics

  • Advanced financial modeling and analytical skills; exceptional Excelproficiencyrequired

  • Experience working with fund data, portfolio reporting, and performance analytics

  • Familiarity with analytics or reporting tools (e.g., SQL, Python, Power BI, Tableau,eFront, Geneva, or similar platforms) preferred

  • Ability to synthesize complex analyses into clear, concise narratives for senior stakeholders

  • Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance or Accounting, Economics, Business, or a related field; advanced degree or CFA a plus

What you can beapartof

You will be joining a team that is genuinely collaborative and whosupporteach other through constant teamwork. Being part of a team that partners with many different business areas and contributes in many ways is exciting.Thisis a high-impact role offering significant visibility across the firm, with the opportunity to influence how fund performance is measured, communicated, andleveragedfor strategic decision-making.

What we offer

  • Full Insurance benefit suite including Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, Vision Insurance, Critical Illness Insurance, Accident Insurance, Short Term Disability, Legal & Identity Theft Insurance, and Pet Insurance.
  • Company paid Life Insurance (option to buy additional available) and Long-Term Disability.
  • Access to benefits concierge service.
  • Access to Mental Health & Well-Being service.
  • 401K:Bridge Investment Group will match your contributions dollar-for-dollar, up to 6% of your pay. These contributions are fully vested immediately. Eligible employees are automatically enrolled at a 4% contribution rate. *The employee must be at least 21 years of age and have worked for the Company for at least 60 days.
  • Paid Time Off: Employee will accrue 5.23 hours of paid time off per pay period for a total of 17 days per year.
  • 11 Paid Holidays per year.
  • Following six (6) months of employment at the Company, you will be eligible per birth, adoption or placement of a child for four (4) weeks of paid parental leave as the primary caregiver to the child or two weeks of paid parental leave as the secondary caregiver to the child. Following two years of employment at the Company, you will be eligible for twelve weeks of paid parental leave per birth, adoption, or placement of a child if you are the primary caregiver of the child.
  • Tuition Reimbursement: Up to $5,000 per year of pre-approved tuition expenses will be reimbursed upon submission of approved documentation. Repayment obligations may apply if employment terminates prior to 24 months. 

We at Bridge acknowledge and appreciate the uniqueness of each individual, understanding that our people are the key to our success. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels respected and valued. Our dedication extends beyond our business goals to making a positive impact in our communities and upholding Sustainability and Responsibility (S&R) principles at our properties and corporate locations. We evaluate all candidates based on merit, without any discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by law. As an equal opportunity employer, we are devoted to creating an inclusive hiring process. We actively work to eliminate barriers and provide reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities. Our goal is to provide fair opportunities and access throughout the recruitment, hiring, and employment experience.

Your specific needs are important to us. If you need accommodations related to a disability or any other protected status during the recruitment process, please email talentsupport@bridgeig.com.

Want to talk with someone about Bridge Culture?

At Bridge, we understand that joining a new workplace is a significant decision. To help you gain deeper insights into our culture, we offer "Culture Conversations." This initiative reflects our commitment to transparency, diversity, and inclusivity.

Culture Conversations are voluntary, one-on-one sessions designed for applicants to connect directly with our diverse staff members. It's your chance to explore the Bridge work environment, ask questions, and get a genuine feel for what it's like to be part of our team.

How it Works:

Culture Conversations are entirely voluntary. Feel free to opt in based on your comfort and curiosity. Choose Your Conversation Partner: We believe in the power of choice. Browse through our diverse group of staff members and select someone you'd like to have a conversation with. This ensures that you connect with someone whose experiences and insights align with your interests. No Impact on the Hiring Process: Your decision to participate or not in Culture Conversations has no bearing on the hiring process. We value your time and references.

Why Participate:
You get to have a firsthand understanding of our workplace culture. Informed decisions lead to fulfilling careers. Culture Conversations empower you with the information needed to make the right choice for your professional journey.

  • Personalized Connection: Connect with an employee who resonates with your professional interests.
  • Inclusivity: Embrace diversity by choosing a conversation partner from various backgrounds and roles.

Make an impact

Bridge Logistics Properties is seeking an Asset Management Analyst to join our team in the East Region. This individual will be conducting financial analysis, preparing investor and internal reporting, monitoring asset performance. They will also assist in business plan execution, tenant relations, and operational efficiency in the region.

  • Asset Performance: Assist in development and implementation of business plans to optimize the asset performance including setting annual budgets, forecasting property level cash flows, and conducting detailed financial analysis to maximize return on investment.
  • Leasing and Tenant Relations: Oversee leasing activity and tenant relations for the East Region. Aid in negotiating new and renewal leases while ensuring lease compliance for existing tenants. Proactively address tenant concerns to ensure tenant satisfaction.
  • Capital Projects & Improvements: Work closely with regional asset manager to oversee capital expenditure projects through coordination with construction teams, vendors, and stakeholders to ensure projects are completed on time, within budget, and to the desired scope.
  • Market Analysis: Conduct market research to assess property values, trends, and competitive positioning.
  • Reporting & Communication: Provide regular performance reports to leadership, including detailed financial analysis, occupancy trends, leasing activity, progress on key initiatives, and quarterly valuations.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with acquisitions, property management, legal, accounting, and finance to optimize portfolio performance.

What you should bring

  • 2 to 4 years of experience in real estate investments or asset management, and general industrial real estate market
  • Effective communicator with ability to manage multiple priorities independently
  • Strong financial modeling and analytical skills

What you can be part of

Joining the Bridge Logistics team, you will be a part of a highly entrepreneurial and fast-paced team that values innovation, autonomy, and excellence. We are a close-knit team-driven environment that offers the opportunity to stand out in an organization experiencing significant growth. The Bridge Logistics team combines its operational and development DNA alongside deep customer, owner, and broker relationships to identify assets with pricing dislocation, an attractive basis, and opportunity for operational upside. Our team's capabilities and extensive footprint within our target markets seek to drive value for both our customers and investors

jobs.title: Associate, Equity Derivatives Trade Services, Equity Derivatives
jobs.description:
Position Description

The position is within Front Office, reporting to Equity Derivatives Department. The role will work directly with the Front Office Trading, Sales, Structurers and Quantitative desks covering trade booking for the Equity Derivatives products.

Key Areas of Responsibility

  • Check automated bookings for Delta One, Flow and Exotic Derivatives Products

  • Trade booking of vanilla instruments, including vanilla equity options, stocks, futures, TRS, IRS, FX (swap/ spot/ NDF) across global underlying

  • Trade booking of Structured Products, including Autocallables, Sharkfin, Boosters, BEN, FCN, ELN etc

  • Process life cycle events, including expiries, early termination, redemption by cash/ physical delivery, coupon pay-outs, corporate action adjustments, etc.

  • Process trade confirmations and term sheets

  • Setup and send valuation reports to clients

  • Prepare periodic/ ad-hoc trade summaries for internal reporting purposes

  • Handle other daily tasks such as PnL reconciliations liaising with Product Control, Operations, Finance

Requirements

  • Degree holder in Mathematics, Statistics, Finance or related discipline(s).

  • Basic understanding of vanilla and exotic products for equities

  • Detail oriented, focus in accuracy and time management

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills

  • Ability to work in a fast paced team environment

  • Willingness to learn and not afraid to take on new tasks

  • Ability to quickly learn new payoffs, booking models and concepts

  • Ideally 3-5 years of Trade Support experience either working with a Trading Desk or in a Middle Office function on the trading desk

  • Any experience in using Calypso is a plus

Stay informed on CITIC CLSA Job Opportunities

Not the right fit? You can create a job alert to receive our latest job openings that meet your interest.

jobs.title: Associate, Index Exotic Trader, Equity Derivatives Trading, Equity Derivatives
jobs.description:
Position Description

As an Index Exotic Options Trader, you will play a key role in expanding the EMEA Index Exotics franchise by working closely with both sales and trading teams. You will partner with traders, quants, and IT to design and enhance risk‑management tools that support the team’s performance and strengthen overall P&L. In addition, you will be responsible for preparing reports on trading opportunities, conducting in‑depth market research, and analysing market conditions to identify both risks and potential sources of value.

Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Risk managing EMEA Exotic on Index books

  • Pricing equity exotic structures for sales and clients

  • Collaborating with traders and quants to develop risk management tools

  • Strong understanding of financial markets, instruments and strategies

  • Knowledge of equity derivatives exotics products

  • Preparing/working on trading opportunities & PnL Attribution & business analysis

  • Conducting market research on potential area of the business expansion

  • Monitoring and analysing market conditions & news & evens to identify risks and opportunities

Requirements

  • Degree holder in Finance, Maths, Engineering or related discipline(s)

  • Good knowledge of Equity Derivatives

  • Minimum of 3 years of Exotic business experience

  • Coding skills are preferred

  • Strong work ethic and motivation

  • Ability to think fast and critically, solve various problems arising from trading, risk and operations

  • Ability to work under pressure and collaborate in a team

Stay informed on CITIC CLSA Job Opportunities

Not the right fit? You can create a job alert to receive our latest job openings that meet your interest.

jobs.title: Equity Derivatives, Listed Trader, Equity Derivatives
jobs.description:
Position Description

As a Listed Trader, you will play a key role in supporting the desk’s trading activities and risk management processes. This position offers exposure to cutting-edge strategies and tools, providing an excellent platform for career growth in a high-energy environment. This is a fast-paced and exciting new role designed for individuals eager to make an impact in the equity derivatives space.

Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Maintain and calibrate volatility surfaces to support accurate option pricing across underlyings.

  • Assist in optimizing trade execution and reconciliation workflows.

  • Build and improve on-desk tools for real-time risk monitoring.

  • Execute trades and manage interactions with counterparties.

  • Support development and testing of proprietary strategies.

  • Collaborate with internal teams to streamline trading processes.

  • Monitor market conditions and liquidity to identify opportunities and manage risk.

  • Analyze option flow and volatility trends to provide insights to senior traders.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher (Master’s preferred) in Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, or a related field.

  • Strong work ethic, self-motivation, and eagerness to learn.

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in Equity Derivatives, ideally with Listed Options trading exposure.

  • Solid understanding of Single Stock and Index Option pricing and volatility models.

  • Strong analytical, quantitative, and interpersonal skills.

  • Ability to perform under pressure and work effectively in a team environment.

  • Excellent communication skills, both written and spoken, in English.

  • Proficient in Python for data analysis (NumPy, Pandas, Polars), SQL/database design, and object-oriented programming with solid grasp of software design patterns

Stay informed on CITIC CLSA Job Opportunities

Not the right fit? You can create a job alert to receive our latest job openings that meet your interest.

jobs.title: Associate Director, Equity Derivatives Structuring, Equity Derivatives
jobs.description:
Position Description
The EQD Structuring role entails marketing EQD products to EMEA clients, providing market analysis and trading ideas, offering indicative pricing, delivering structured product solutions, collaborating with various teams to improve client service systems, and coordinating global business strategies. The ideal candidate should have a degree in Finance, Mathematics, Sciences, Engineering, or a related discipline, preferably with coding skills. Strong communication, problem-solving abilities, and the ability to work under pressure and collaborate in a team are essential for success in this role.


Key Areas of Responsibilities
•    Marketing EQD products to EMEA clients including Structured Note & Swap & certificates
•    Preparing market colour and trading ideas to EQD clients
•    Providing indicative pricing to existing and prospective clients
•    Providing structured product solutions to EQD clients
•    Working very closely with trading/sales/quant/prime service in improving our system for a better client service
•    Collaborating with global structuring team to coordinate global business push


Requirements
•    Degree holder in Finance, Mathematics, Sciences, Engineering or related discipline(s)
•    Coding skills are preferred
•    Good communication skills
•    Strong work ethic and motivation
•    Ability to think fast and critically, solve various problems arising from trading, risk and operations 
•    Ability to work under pressure and collaborate in a team

jobs.title: Investment Counsellor, Wealth Management
jobs.description:
Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Provide client portfolio advisory to private wealth clients on multi-asset classes including equities, fixed incomes, funds, and structured products based on house view.

  • Formulate investment strategies that align with client’s target strategic assets allocation to meet investment objectives and risk profile.

  • Partner sales teams on client meetings or social engagements to articulate investment ideas, investment concepts, create investment proposals and conduct timely portfolio reviews.

  • Act as the second point of contact by offering market views, planning and execution of investment strategies to private wealth clients.

  • Monitor client portfolios for performance, appropriateness and investment opportunities.

  • Keep abreast of new products developments in the market, propose new product ideas, further evaluate and promote ideas approved and endorsed by the products management team.

  • Ensure that proposed ideas and strategies to clients are closely followed-up with monitoring of market changes and developments that may impact the clients, and give timely advice on the investment portfolios.

  • Ensure adherence to internal and external regulations and policies at all times.

  • Ensure integrity of sales process and compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.

  • Build a highly proactive and long-term relationship with clients by generating and sustaining revenue growth whilst broadening client base.

Requirements

  • Bachelor degree major in Finance, Business Administration, Economics or related disciplines.

  • Chartered Financial Analyst holder or professional qualification of FRM / CPA is preferred.

  • Minimum 4 years of experience in financial industry in an investment advisory position preferably in Private Banking / Wealth Management specializing in sales and marketing of investment products, dealing experience will be an advantage.

  • Extensive knowledge of investment products including equities, fixed incomes, funds, structured products, FX, options, futures etc.

  • Client focused with strong business acumen, presentation (both written and oral), and good relationship building skills.

  • Pleasant interpersonal and communications skills.

  • Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills with a future-oriented mindset ready to innovate and adapt to changes.

  • Clear understanding of inter-dependencies and synergies within the team, and able to collaborate effectively across departments.

  • Ability to thrive in a fast paced environment and remain empathetic, passionate and resilient to both clients’ demands and rapid market changes.

  • Good command of written and spoken English.

  • Must be proficient in spoken Mandarin, particularly in product offering concepts and explanation to Greater China clients.

  • Proficient in written Mandarin communications to clients if necessary.

  • Possess CACS 1 & 2 certifications, and the relevant CMFAS licensing.

jobs.title: Trader, Treasury
jobs.description:
Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Execute trades across FX, interest rates, bonds, repos, securities borrowing/lending (SBL), and precious metals, ensuring both pre- and post-trade compliance.
  • Requite to monitor and trade during London and New York market hours.
  • Conduct research and development of cross-currency (XCCY), interest rate, credit, precious metal, and repo trading strategies.
  • Monitor and manage treasury trading books, as well as Group exposures in rates, FX, and credit.
  • Contribute to the development of in-house trading platforms and algorithmic trading strategies.
  • Facilitate trading arrangements with counterparties and oversee counterparty trading limits.
  • Support the launch of new product initiatives.
  • Liaise with operations teams to ensure smooth settlement processes.
  • Participate in ISDA, GMRA, and GMSLA negotiations, and assist with collateral management.

Requirements

  • 2–5 years’ experience in global markets trading, gained on either the buy side or sell side.
  • Strong interest and passion for trading in global markets, with the ability to perform effectively under pressure.
  • Solid understanding of credit, interest rate, FX, and precious metals products.
  • Familiarity with ISDA, GMRA, and GMSLA documentation and processes.
  • Proficient in Bloomberg Terminal, AIM, and TOM.
  • Knowledge of Python and VBA programming is an advantage.
  • Bachelor’s degree in quantitative finance, Economics, Engineering, or Mathematics.
  • Excellent command of spoken and written English, Mandarin, and Cantonese.
  • Strong communication skills; responsible, self-motivated, and eager to learn.

jobs.title: Quantitative Product Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

We are seeking a dynamic and client-focused Quantitative Product Specialist for the Quantitative Investment Science (QIS) team. This role involves connecting with clients and prospects, driving adoption of proprietary quantitative tools, and collaborating with internal teams to provide data-driven insights that improve investment decisions. The ideal candidate presents ideas clearly, possesses strong quantitative knowledge, and enjoys delivering impactful solutions.

What you will do

Strengthen client and prospect engagement:

  • Collaborate with Investor Relations and Client Service to actively involve clients and prospects using QIS tools, presenting them as key resources for manager selection and portfolio construction.
  • Host product demos and facilitate deeper conversations with investment experts to highlight QIS as a strategic differentiator.
  • Contribute to lead generation and define success metrics for product engagement and team interactions.
  • Build educational materials, commentary, standard explanatory content, talking points, and presentations that simplify complex quantitative concepts for diverse internal and external audiences.

Drive adoption of QIS tools and solutions:

  • Join forces with investment teams to apply detailed, data-driven insights in decision-making processes, boosting efficiency and reinforcing strategies for stronger outcomes.
  • Build stakeholder buy‑in through tailored engagements, impactful presentations, and demonstrations that showcase how QIS solutions elevate investment processes.

Lead Marketing Strategy & Execution:

  • Work with marketing teams to develop compelling product materials, presentations, insights, and thought leadership content showcasing QIS capabilities.
  • Support investor communications by translating quantitative concepts into clear, client-friendly messaging.

Product Development and Competitor Intelligence:

  • Partner with Product Management to refine product roadmap based on market trends and investor feedback.
  • Capture competitive intelligence and find opportunities for product differentiation.
  • Collaborate with marketing to host and attend industry events, broadening QIS brand exposure.
  • Operations & Reporting:
  • Define, measure and communicate important metrics to key internal collaborators on QIS platform engagement.
  • Improve cross-functional partnerships and integrate QIS efforts into firm-wide processes.
  • Plug into current workflows and systems to build repeatable, scalable processes that improve efficiency and impact.

What you bring

  • Analytical approach with strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to balance technical rigor with creative marketing approaches.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to build relationships and convey complex concepts clearly.
  • Collaborative teammate with strong interpersonal skills.
  • Strategic problem solver with a client-centric approach.
  • Passion for defining new processes, systems, and guidelines to scale support practices.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, or related field preferred; CFA or CAIA designation a plus.
  • 5+ years of experience in institutional relationship management or business development, private equity, asset management, or related financial services role with a quantitative focus preferred.
  • Demonstrated ability to communicate complex analytics to non-technical audiences; strong presentation and writing skills.
  • Experience in marketing strategy and content development within financial services is a plus.
  • Strong proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, Python, R) and financial modeling a plus.

#LI-Hybrid

Salary Range

$136,000.00 - $204,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: Vice President, Evergreen Private Equity Investment Specialist (Boston)
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

Except for work travel, this position is expected to be in the Boston office 4 days minimum per week. There will also be a need for travel.

About the role:

Vice President, Investment Specialist will work to drive the growth of HarbourVest’s private equity evergreen solutions in the United States.

HarbourVest’s evergreen solutions include private markets comingled vehicles and separate account opportunities, targeted at private wealth and institutional investors. Responsibilities include working alongside Investor Relations and Private Wealth teams to facilitate new business wins, identifying capability builds that cross-functionally improve the product and identifying adjacencies for new product development. This individual will be responsible for educating and engaging with the global Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams and other internal partners; making pitches to clients and prospects; conducting portfolio reviews with clients and overseeing important client responses to ensure quality of submissions.

The ideal candidate is someone who has:

  • A strong understanding of private market evergreen vehicles and a deep knowledge of private equity and the private wealth market.
  • Excellent presentation and client engagement skills with the ability to tailor information based on the audience.
  • Strong relationship management skills to partner closely with fellow HarbourVest Investment and Investor Relations professionals, as well as external prospects and clients.
  • Strong investment and analytical competence.
  • Strong written skills.
  • A high level of maturity and ability to multi-task with little direct supervision.
  • An entrepreneurial approach with an ability to be forward-thinking and work without a playbook.
  • A desire for travel.
  • An understanding of the investment process.
  • A strong understanding of quantitative investment measurements and statistical tools.

What you will do:

External responsibilities:

  • Support sales, by bringing in-depth vehicle and market expertise to prospect and client meetings.
  • Support the Investor Relations teams in their discussions with private wealth prospects, clients, distribution partners and institutional investors. Demonstrate deep knowledge of the market, product, HarbourVest’s capabilities, solution and differentiation.
  • Take into account the broader picture; engage externally around a broad range of business and investment topics. Speak with nuance around the investment landscape, client portfolio and market outlook.
  • Display creativity and problem solving in proposing solutions to client challenges. Consider the wider business implications and context (complexity, operational impact) in discussions that will result in the optimal outcome for HarbourVest.
  • Deep understanding of key competitors, fundraising and industry trends.

Internal product specialist responsibilities:

  • Engage with the Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams to collect feedback from the market. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence and apply it to product evolution and content development.
  • Project manage the sales opportunity set and go-to-market strategy for US private equity evergreen products. Act as liaison point for all evergreen collective sales efforts, organized across various teams for the product.
  • Partner with the Investor Relations team to serve as the main point of contact to arrange calls/meetings, follow-ups, comprehensive investment knowledge, identity and address concerns and provide guidance through diligence.
  • Educate the Private Client and Investor Relations teams on the product and strategy.
  • Oversee important RFPs, written collateral, portfolio updates and client responses to ensure the quality of submissions.

Education Preferred:

Strong academic and professional credentials. MBA and / or CFA preferred but not required.

Experience:

10+ years of investment or product roles with a client service orientation. An investment research background is a plus. Must have a blend of product leadership, investment capabilities and commercial sense. Relevant backgrounds include product specialist for liquid products, investment banking, equity analyst, consulting or transaction services experience. Spanish language fluency is an asset.

Evergreen Solutions Team Description

Guided by a clear and ambitious mission, the Evergreen Solutions Team is enabling HarbourVest to become the leading multimanager provider of private markets focused evergreen solutions for wealth and institutional investors globally. Our team blends deep investment expertise with industry‑leading data, technology, and portfolio management capabilities to deliver seamless, scalable solutions for institutions and wealth partners. We collaborate across HarbourVest to build purpose‑built tools, streamline operations, and deliver transparent insights that support thoughtful allocation and long‑term portfolio construction.

#LI-Hybrid

Salary Range

$117,000.00 - $163,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed

HarbourVest is currently seeking candidates for an Associate position in the EMEA Primary Investment Group based in London. As an Associate, you will be responsible for evaluating fund investment opportunities across the Private Equity, Growth Equity and Venture Capital markets. As part of the evaluation process, you will be responsible for meeting with managers who are raising funds, creating detailed performance analytics including benchmarking and portfolio company underwriting, forming and articulating investment theses to our senior team, conducting reference calls with portfolio companies and presenting opportunities to our global Investment Committee.

The ideal candidate is someone who has:

  • Strong analytical capabilities with ability to synthesize recommendations based on analysis with strong attention to detail
  • Strong quantitative skills with deep experience in Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint)
  • Good knowledge of private equity markets and financial expertise
  • Proven leadership skills and ability to succeed working independently and in collaborative teams
  • Ability to build strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal

What you will do:

  • Perform due diligence on Private Equity, Growth Equity and Venture Capital managers and fund opportunities, including preparing detailed performance and benchmarking analyses, participating in manager diligence meetings, making reference calls, and conducting independent research
  • Source and evaluate new Private Equity, Growth Equity and Venture Capital fund opportunities.
  • Develop investment theses and present key findings to senior professionals.
  • Prepare diligence materials and present opportunities to the global Investment Committee
  • Complete special projects for internal usage and client requests, including industry research and market updates.
  • Prepare materials for HarbourVest's Annual Meeting and other client meetings
  • Monitor primary fund investments through attendance at annual meetings, reviewing quarterly and annual reports, and participating in portfolio update calls as required. Build and enhance relationships with fund managers
  • Source and refer interesting direct equity, credit and secondary opportunities internally and facilitate/assist with the initial assessment of these opportunities
  • Work to build and expand your personal network and knowledge base through continuing to meet and work with professionals at HarbourVest
     

What you bring:

  • 2-3 years of strong professional experience in private equity, asset management, manager research, investment consulting, investment banking or related financial services
  • Demonstrated excellence in analytical reasoning, quantitative analysis, and verbal/written communications in team-based settings
  • Demonstrated interest in Primary fund investing

Education Preferred:

  • Bachelors degree or equivalent experience

Experience:

2-3 years of professional experience in private equity manager research, investment consulting, investment banking or related financial services

  • An entrepreneurial approach with an ability to be forward-thinking and work without a playbook.
  • A desire for travel.
  • An understanding of the investment process.
  •  A strong understanding of quantitative investment measurements and statistical tools.
     

What you will do:
 

External responsibilities:

  • Support Investor Relations and Consultant Relations by bringing in-depth product and market expertise to meetings with clients and prospects. Demonstrate deep knowledge of HarbourVest’s capabilities, solutions and differentiators.
  • Take into account the broader picture; engage externally around a broad range of business and investment topics. Speak with nuance around the investment landscape, client portfolios and market outlook.
  • Display creativity and problem solving in proposing solutions to client challenges. Consider the wider business implications and context (complexity, operational impact) in discussions that will result in the optimal outcome for HarbourVest.
  • Deep understanding of key competitors, fundraising and industry trends.
     

Internal product management responsibilities:

  • Engage with Investor Relations and Consultant Relations to collect feedback from the market. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence and apply it content development.
  • Project manage the fundraising pipeline and go-to-market strategy. Organize sales efforts across various regional teams.
  • Partner with Investor Relations and Consultant Relations to serve as the main point of contact to arrange calls/ meetings, follow-ups, provide comprehensive investment knowledge, identity and address concerns and provide guidance through diligence.
  • Educate Investor Relations and Consultant Relations on key market themes and competitive dynamics affecting the product and strategy.
  • Oversee and lead the development and enhancement of product marketing content including presentations, factsheets, webcast materials, client update materials, and written communications.
  • Be responsible for and own the development and improvement of product RFPs, written collateral, portfolio updates and client responses to ensure the quality of submissions.
  • Review and sign-off on client reporting.
  • Lead all aspects of the development and improvement of all internal product materials including competitor analysis, internal talking points and internal fundraising updates.
     

Education Preferred:
 

Strong academic and professional credentials. MBA and / or CFA preferred but not required.
 

Experience:
 

15+ years of investment or product roles with a client service orientation. Must have a blend of product leadership, investment capabilities and commercial sense. Relevant backgrounds include product specialist for private market offerings, investment banking, equity analyst, consulting or transaction-related experience.

Salary Range

$117,000.00 - $163,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

companyDetails.description: For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

jobs.title: Infrastructure Equity Intern
jobs.description:
Job Title:                               Asia Pacific Infrastructure Intern

Location:                               Singapore

Department:                        Infrastructure Equity

Reporting to:                        Asia Pacific Infrastructure Director

What the Hiring Manager says….

“An exciting opportunity to gain invaluable experience in the world of private equity investments, specifically within the exciting and impactful realm of Infrastructure with a primary focus on the dynamic and rapidly evolving Energy Transition sector. This internship is designed for highly motivated individuals who are eager to learn, contribute, and be part of an entrepreneurial and supportive team”

Position Summary

ICG is looking for an Infrastructure Intern for a 6-month internship to start in June/July 2026. As a member of the Private Equity (Infrastructure) team, you will assist with the evaluation and execution of investment opportunities for ICG. You will gain exposure to the diverse infrastructure sector particularly in the energy transition sub-sector as well as expand their knowledge and develop skills in evaluating private equity investments.

Primary Responsibilities

You will have the unique opportunity to work actively on all aspects of potential investments as well as fundraising efforts. The responsibilities of the role include, but are not limited to:

  • Gather and synthesize industry and market data

  • Evaluate potential investment’s financial and operating performance to the overall industry and market trends

  • Build, review and operate complex financial models used for evaluating investments

  • Assist with drafting investment memorandums and presentations (highlighting and explaining industry trends, investment overview and thesis, key risks/issues, etc.)

  • Assist with drafting of fundraising documents

Key Requirements/ Qualifications

You will have demonstrable experience of: 

  • Genuine enthusiasm and curiosity for investing

  • Unquestioned integrity, strong work ethic

  • Ability to take initiative, work both independently and within teams, willingness to learn

  • A supportive and adaptable team member who can build positive relationships

  • Financial modeling skills

  • Proficient with Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel

  • Penultimate/final year undergraduate students are preferred

Our Global Benefits Framework is centred around these 3 key attributes:

  • Wellbeing - Supporting your mental, physical and financial wellbeing as well as your lifestyle needs.

  • Inclusivity – Fit for purpose and competitive, no matter who or where you are.

  • Longevity – Long Term and sustainable in design.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Fund Analyst (Real Estate)
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:

  • Company-provided healthcare

  • A competitive annual leave allowance

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements

  • Generous pension provisions

  • Income protection

  • Health and wellness benefits

  • Volunteering days

  • Enhanced parental leave

  • Life insurance

What we are looking for

We’re looking for a Senior Associate, Fund Analyst to join our London‑based Real Estate Investment team—a role offering exceptional exposure across the full investment lifecycle!

This is an opportunity to work on acquisitions, asset management, structured finance, fund strategy, and investor reporting across pan‑European equity and debt investments.


Beyond core analytical work, you’ll also contribute to Wealth Solutions initiatives, supporting bespoke deal structuring and new product development for private wealth clients. If you're ready to blend rigorous investment analysis with strategic thinking and client‑focused innovation, this role is for you.

You'll be responsible for:

As a key member of the investment team, you’ll deliver high‑quality analysis, build advanced financial models, and help drive decisions that influence the direction of our European real estate strategies.

Investment Analysis & Underwriting

  • Build and maintain sophisticated Excel‑based models for acquisitions, dispositions, and portfolio forecasting.

  • Underwrite new equity and debt investment opportunities and validate external models.

  • Prepare investment memoranda, transaction analyses, and fund strategy papers for internal and external stakeholders.

Portfolio & Asset Management

  • Monitor and forecast performance of existing investments, including IRR updates and variance analysis.

  • Work with asset managers and JV partners on annual business plans and hold/sell recommendations.

  • Support fund strategy through ongoing portfolio-level analysis.

Market Research & Due Diligence

  • Conduct market research and compile qualitative and quantitative insights.

  • Analyze macro trends, sector dynamics, and competitive positioning across European markets.

Business Development & Wealth Solutions

  • Support bespoke deal structuring for private wealth clients.

  • Assist in developing innovative product concepts for multi‑investor platforms.

  • Prepare tailored materials and presentations for wealth management partners.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Partner with structured finance, tax, and fund accounting teams to ensure financial models reflect capital structures and tax considerations.

  • Build strong working relationships with internal teams, transaction partners, and JV stakeholders.

You'll need to have:

  • Solid experience in real estate private equity, investment banking, or advisory. Exposure to pan‑European real estate markets is a plus.

  • Strong Excel financial modeling skills and the ability to manage multiple workstreams in a fast‑paced environment.

  • Sharp analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex financial and operational data.

  • Strong understanding of real estate fundamentals, valuation, and key performance drivers.

  • Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, or a related field.
    CFA, RICS, or a relevant postgraduate qualification is an advantage.

  • Clear, concise communicator who can simplify complex ideas.

  • Collaborative team player comfortable working across international offices.

  • Able to translate data into actionable insights and informed recommendations.

  • Brings sound judgment, context, and perspective beyond the numbers.


Disability Confident Scheme

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the ‘minimum criteria’ for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at EMEA-TalentAcquisition@invesco.com confirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.

jobs.title: Senior Manager / Associate Director, HK Institutional & Pension Business (Hong Kong Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

The primary role of Senior Manager/Associate Director is responsible to develop and close new business, sales generation in Hong Kong and Macau for complex institutional and pension accounts.

This will involve developing new leads, preparing RFPs, giving presentations to prospective clients, ensuring client and prospects receive full and efficient service and promotes products managed by our office and to extend to other offices within the Group.

Looking for highly motivated candidate with solid industry connection as well as knowledge, who will be able to identify and source deals and to handle the entire Sales process.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Develop new Institutional & Pension business opportunities in Hong Kong and Macau with key prospects, clients and to liaise with related consultants. 
  • Identify potential clients, to manage preparation of presentations, to follow up with the client contacts from sales of investment services to deal close.
  • Evaluate on detailed analysis such as asset liabilities, data research and analysis, investment risk return profile, etc. in particular for pension and insurance clients.
  • Develop in-depth knowledge of Institutional clients, understand their business needs and to effectively assist them in decision making process.
  • Partner with regional and global investment centers to shape the core offerings (traditional long-only strategies, alternatives, multi-assets, insurance solutions, passively managed instruments & indexing capabilities) to cater for different client needs to maximize growth.
  • Generate ideas and tactics, and to coordinate with RFP team to develop innovative responses for the deals.
  • Contribute ideas for new products which will be suitable to the markets and business segments assigned.
  • Achieve activity statistics for the region.
  • Lead or participate in other ad-hoc projects as assigned.
  • Maintains good relationship with all internal stakeholders whilst to understand and to cater for client needs.
  • Contribution in helping the team to reach the leadership position objective with the right optimal approach to meet the needs of Invesco as well as to the clienteles.
  • Ensure that procedures and compliance requirements are compiled.

Requirement for the Role: 

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent – preferably in Finance or Investments related.
  • Relevant professional licenses of the Asset Management industry.
  • At least 6+ years of total relevant working experience
  • Deep knowledge and experience of Institutional clients in Hong Kong and Macau.
  • Strong experience within Insurance segment is a plus
  • Able to think strategically and out-of-the-box
  • Creative in coming up with new ideas in response to prospects’ needs
  • Experience in servicing clients and dealing with sophisticated institutional clients at a senior level
  • Good time management skills
  • Well organized and results focused
  • Ability to multi-task across strategic and tactical work tasks
  • Accurate, articulate, presentable and showing initiative

How to Apply:

  •  Apply for the role directly atInvesco Careers:Careers at Invesco | APAC.

To know more about us:

  • About Invesco:https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/home.html

  • About our Culture:https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/about-us/our-culture.html

  • About our CR program:https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/our-commitments/corporate-responsibility.html

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

jobs.title: Associate, Investment Management
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Associate partners with Asset Managers in the management and oversight of various commercial and multifamily real estate investments, with the overall goal of optimizing asset performance and values. The Associate plays an integral role in developing and executing property specific strategies, analyzing major leasing decisions and how they impact value, performing major feasibility studies and evaluating property level performance against relevant industry benchmarks. In addition, the role is responsible for providing support and analysis for the preparation of the quarterly and annual property reports and budgets for a portfolio of assets.

SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Work with Asset Manager to create property level operational strategies and analyze major leases, capital projects or other investment factors impacting asset value.

  • Assist in the review of valuation models (Argus and Excel) and take part in the acquisition and disposition process, overseeing the flow of due diligence information for select investments as needed.

  • Prepare financial analysis to track historic portfolio and property level results in comparison to industry benchmarks.

  • Coordinate the development of Annual Business Plans and Quarterly reports by assisting in the preparation of property operating and capital budgets and reviewing and approving on-going financial variance reporting.   

  • Communicate with third party property management and leasing personnel as well as insurance and real estate tax consultants and other third party providers as needed.

  • Review lease submittal packages, evaluate prospective tenant businesses and credit worthiness, and perform other lease related financial analysis as needed.

  • Collect, analyze and synthesize relevant market reports and other lease or transaction related data.  

  • Help facilitate lender compliance with asset specific requirements such as obtaining approvals for lease agreements or significant capital expenditure projects and coordinating the release of escrow accounts.

  • Support enforcement of internal audit requirements with third party providers, and further ensure that such firms are conducting business in accordance with property management agreements.

POSITION QUALIFICATIONS

Education:

  • Four year or equivalent undergraduate degree in Business or related field. Post graduate education a plus.  A commitment to continued education in real estate will be encouraged.

Work Experience:

  • 2 – 5 years of experience in the commercial and/or multi-family real estate industry (or business related activity) is required.

  • Property level experience is a plus.

Skills:

  • Effective interpersonal, communication (verbal and written) and listening skills.

  • Strong computer literacy including fluency with Argus, VTS, RealPage, Microsoft Excel and Word, and PowerPoint.

  • Ability to understand and interpret commercial lease agreements and language as well as present value concepts and financial terminology.

  • Analytical problem solving skills capable to undertake detailed analyses and make well-reasoned, decisive recommendations.

  • Demonstrated prioritization, organization and time management skills necessary to balance competing deadlines.

  • Desire to multi-task in a complex, high-volume environment and competency to work with all levels of management and personnel on critical projects.

Travel:

  • Some travel will be required for periodic asset management related property visits and select acquisition due diligence

#MTIVZ

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $110,000 to $120,000 annually.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Asset-Backed Investments
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $40 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT. 

The Senior Analyst will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning public and private asset-backed investments across Invesco-managed products. They will also participate in business development activities with both current and prospective clients. Additionally, the Senior Analyst will be actively involved in elevating the team profile across the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform.

Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate with portfolio managers and other analysts on a variety of public and private asset-backed finance investment strategies managed by the Structured Investments team, including mutual funds, exchange-traded funds and institutional accounts

  • Deep experience in asset-backed underwriting which includes, but is not limited to, assessing underlying collateral and conducting loan and bond cash flow modeling

  • Underwrite public ABS and private asset-backed finance investments across a wide range of ABS/ABF sectors and issuers. The team invests in flow, liquid credit, esoteric, and non-public sectors

  • Conduct market research and assist senior team members in formulating an investment outlook

  • Utilize excel, general-purpose programming languages and artificial intelligence to conduct monthly credit surveillance/monitoring

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of public and private structured products (ABS, CMBS, MBS, asset-backed loans and mortgage-backed loans) as well as other fixed income sectors

  • Develop meaningful relationships with loan originators and sell-side contacts to help facilitate deal flow and best execution on investment acquisitions

  • Interact with broader Structured Investments analysts to better understand underwriting, internal rating processes and sector nuances

  • Formulate and communicate trade ideas for the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform to generate potential alpha for the wider set of investment teams

  • Periodically interact with current or prospective retail and institutional investors

  • Draft periodic investment commentary for both internal and external audiences

  • Commercialize asset-backed investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of experience in underwriting asset-backed securities or private asset-backed finance investments across a variety of collateral types (e.g., credit cards, autos, student loans, whole business, aircraft, container, data center, fiber optic, etc.)

  • Expertise in the use of Intex and Bloomberg

  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office, including Excel and PowerPoint

  • Python and VBA programming skills are a plus

  • CFA Charterholder or pursuit thereof preferred   

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset, with desire to assist in growth of the ABS and private asset-backed finance platform

  • Open minded and willingness to learn new topics and investment/asset subsectors

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Highly methodical with a strong attention to detail

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Ability to source investments and drive outcomes through decisive risk-taking

Full time

Employee

Yes

This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.
