jobs.title: Investment Analyst, Real Estate
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Investment Analyst role in the Real Estate group is ideal for individuals passionate about pursuing a career in real estate. This role will provide exposure to the key areas of our Real Estate portfolio – investments, asset management and portfolio management. The main responsibilities will focus on analyzing potential real estate investment opportunities, executing on transactions, tracking business plans and reporting on investment performance.

Who you'll work with

This role will work in a dynamic team-based environment in Dallas, directly supporting senior team members where they will have the chance to define and differentiate yourself and build strategic relationships within the global market. The Real Estate team comprises nearly 40 investment professionals and invests and manages assets across a geographically diverse portfolio of approximately C$28 billion with assets in North America, South America, Europe and Asia, including Australia.

What you'll do

Investments and Asset Management

  • Build and maintain detailed financial models to evaluate potential investment opportunities.

  • Conduct market research and competitive analysis to support acquisition and disposition decisions.

  • Assist in the due diligence process and incorporate the findings into a financial model.

  • Facilitate sale processes, including preparing materials, engaging with stakeholders, and ensuring timely execution

  • Contribute to investment memos and presentation materials.

  • Support ongoing asset management initiatives, including monitoring investments, tracking business plans and executing key priorities.

Portfolio Management

  • Prepare data-driven portfolio analytics for key portfolio monitoring and reporting initiatives.

  • Measure investment performance by developing an understanding of OTPP returns, benchmark, and risk frameworks to assess historical and projected performance.

  • Support long-term capital planning by preparing deployment forecasts, as well as monitoring short-term liquidity and deployment activity.

  • Support the valuation process, including tracking valuation results and preparing management reporting.

  • Build relationships with other key functions across OTPP and contribute to Fund-wide initiatives.

  • Facilitate sale processes, including preparing materials, engaging with stakeholders, and ensuring timely execution

  • Contribute to investment memos and presentation materials.

  • Support ongoing asset management initiatives, including monitoring investments, tracking business plans and executing key priorities.

What you'll need

  • Undergraduate degree required in business, finance, accounting, or related field.

  • Up to one year of experience, preferably in investment banking, principal investing or investment advisory.

  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

  • Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint.

  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth financial modelling analysis.

  • Strong time-management skills, with the ability to balance multiple priorities under demanding timelines, while remaining meticulous.

  • Proactive attitude, demonstrated ability and desire to work in a vigorous, fast-paced environment.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

jobs.title: Intern - Investments (Jul - Dec 2026)
jobs.description:
The deadline to apply for this role is:

Ontario Teachers' is Canada's largest single-profession pension plan with $269.6 billion in net assets. We pay pensions and invest plan assets on behalf of 343,000 working and retired teachers. Since our establishment as an independent organization in 1990, we have built an international reputation for innovation and leadership in investment management and member services. Teachers’ is an experienced global investor and a significant investor in the Asia Pacific region.

Ontario Teachers’ is headquartered in Toronto and has investment offices based in London, Singapore, India, and San Francisco. Ontario Teachers’ employs more than 1,500 employees globally.

The Opportunity

Are you curious to learn more about becoming an investment professional? Do you thrive in a fast-paced working environment? Do you want to join a vibrant team with a mandate to grow? If so, join the Ontario Teachers’ student program! We are looking for dedicated self-starters who share our passion for investing and want to learn how we execute on investment opportunities and manage our existing investments across the different asset-classes.

Infrastructure & Natural Resources

The Infrastructure & Natural Resources (“INR”) Group invests in global infrastructure and natural resources companies to deliver growing, inflation-linked cash flows over a long-term investment horizon. Our global team consists of over 70 investment professionals handle a geographically diverse CAD 40 billion global investment portfolio. Our investments are primarily through direct, privately held, controlling or co-controlling stakes, rather than through funds or passive holdings.

Private Capital

OTPP’s Private Capital (PC) team leads the Plan’s private equity activities. With over C$60 billion of AUM globally, Private Capital invests directly and through private equity funds in companies around the world seeking to grow and add value over the long run. Private Capital stands as one of the largest and most sophisticated pools of capital in the world. Through over 200+ direct and fund investments, Private Capital has generated a net internal rate of return of roughly 20% since inception.

 What you’ll do

  • You will be involved in assisting the team in analysing potential new investment opportunities, managing our existing investments and will gain the following hands-on experience and training:

  • Hone your financial modelling abilities by analysing investment opportunities

  • Learn to identify key value drivers and risks for investments and perform related sensitivity and scenario analyses

  • Support due diligence exercises

  • Prepare valuations, presentations, and reports to senior investment professionals

  • Develop and analyse business cases for portfolio company / asset value creation initiatives (debt refinancings, expansion projects, management compensation, on-going budgeting, etc.)

What you’ll need

  • Currently pursuing an undergraduate with a concentration in investments, finance, or accounting

  • Previous intern experience in M&A, investment banking, private equity, equity research, INR, or real estate.

  • Advanced skills in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) required

  • Familiarity with financial modelling – you are a number whiz! 

  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth analysis

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills – you are a people person and love working in a dynamic team!

  • Ability to balance multiple priorities and initiatives while remaining detail-oriented

  • Currently enrolled in your 3rd / 4th year

  • You will be returning to your studies after the work term is completed

  • Availability for 6 months internship from Jul 2026 to Dec2026.

If you require an accommodation for the application, recruitment or interview process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

#LI-CN1 , #LI-INITIALS

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development, including lunch and learns, networking and online learning 

  • Regular student-led events – be part of a global student network across our offices!

jobs.title: Managing Director, Teachers' Venture Growth - North America Direct Investing
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan (“Teachers’”) manages ~C$270B globally and is recognized as one of the most sophisticated direct investors in the world. With over 80% of assets managed internally, we combine long-term capital with disciplined underwriting and the ability to lead at scale. Teachers’ Venture Growth (TVG) is our dedicated late-stage venture and growth equity platform, managing ~C$15B globally. On the direct investment front, we deploy ~C$1B annually into minority investments in scaled private technology companies. TVG has invested in category leaders such as SpaceX, Databricks, Canva, Anthropic, Gusto, Grafana, Kong, StackAdapt, and many others.

We are seeking a Managing Director in San Francisco to join our North America Direct team. We are intentionally looking to add a Silicon Valley-based investor who brings differentiated access, cycle-tested judgment, and the ambition to help scale a high-performing institutional growth platform.

The Mandate

You will:

  • Own sector coverage within North American technology

  • Develop differentiated investment theses

  • Originate, lead, and close minority growth investments (typically Series C +)

  • Write and defend investment committee recommendations

  • Serve as an active board member or observer

  • Develop junior talent and elevate team performance

TVG’s average initial equity commitment is ~US$100M and we frequently lead or co-lead rounds. You will collaborate closely with senior TVG leadership, the TVG Funds team, and adjacent OTPP asset classes, but investment decisions and portfolio ownership resides within TVG.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first 12–24 months, you will have:

  • Expanded our access to high-quality companies and founders not already in our network

  • Led and closed compelling later-stage investments

  • Demonstrated strong board engagement and value-add through inflection points

  • Raised the bar for analytical rigor and decision quality across the team

  • Developed junior investors through direct coaching and meaningful ownership

We are building the next phase of our North America platform and this role is expected to contribute materially to that evolution.

The Profile

We are seeking an investor who brings:

  • 12+ years of direct investing experience in later-stage venture or growth equity

  • A demonstrated track record leading minority investments in scaled technology companies

  • Meaningful board experience and exposure through exit

  • Deep, credible relationships across Silicon Valley and the broader North American technology ecosystem

  • Strong transaction leadership and negotiation capabilities

We will not consider candidates whose experience is limited to early-stage investing or those without direct underwriting responsibility.

Equally important:

  • Experience investing through multiple market cycles with visible evidence of judgment shaped by volatility

  • High standards, intellectual independence, and accountability

  • A collaborative, strengths-based leadership style suited to a global institutional platform

We focus primarily on later-stage technology assets across a wide range of sectors, however, we are generally less active in consumer internet and health tech.

Why Teachers’

Teachers’ offers something distinctive in Silicon Valley:

  • Long-duration capital that allows us to invest through cycles

  • The ability to write large checks and lead

  • A disciplined, globally integrated investment culture

  • A truly global platform with deep institutional resources

  • A clear purpose: delivering retirement security to 340,000 teachers who rely on us for long-term financial stability in retirement

Our capital is not transient. It represents the deferred earnings of educators. That responsibility shapes how we underwrite risk, support companies, and build enduring value. This is an opportunity to combine institutional scale with entrepreneurial impact.

Compensation

Base salary range: USD $300,000–$380,000, plus eligibility for Teachers’ annual incentive plan and Long Term Incentive Plan compensation programs. Final compensation reflects experience, capability, and internal alignment.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

At Ontario Teachers', diversity is one of our core strengths. We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring.  We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves.  To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Valuation (12-month contract)
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Senior Associate, Valuation will participate in the planning and execution of the semi-annual and year end private investment valuation processes, for the purpose of OTPP’s semi-annual and year end financial statement reporting.

Who you'll work with

The Senior Associate will report to the Senior Manager, Valuation.

What you'll do

Supporting the group with the planning and execution of the semi-annual and year-end valuation processes

  • Assist with the review of “fair value” valuations prepared by OTPP’s Investment Divisions and the preparation of valuation analysis and summary memoranda in respect thereof

  • Review of fair value valuations prepared by external appraisers and valuation advisors

  • Help develop recommendations of fair value to be used for the purposes of OTPP’s financial statements

  • Support the development, tracking and reporting of plans and timelines for assigned investment portfolios to ensure an effective and efficient valuation process

Support the management of OTPP’s use of external advisors employed in the private investment valuation process and the management of the corresponding relationships, including:

  • Ensuring internal information required by external advisors is provided by both the Investment Division and Finance in accordance with established timelines

  • Monitoring and reporting on progress of external advisor deliverables

  • Critiquing external advisor reports in ensuring their findings are reflective of best practices and are consistent with the definition of fair value

  • Review and incorporation of external advisor recommendations and findings into OTPP’s conclusions

  • Communication of issues noted to the managers, where necessary, on a timely basis

Help ensure OTPP’s valuation policies and practices remain best practice

  • Assist in the review of internal methodologies and processes for assigned investments with a view to continuous process improvement

  • Monitoring external best practice standards (e.g. IPEV Valuation Guidelines) and practices

  • Support the annual review and update of OTPP’s private investment valuation policies including recommending changes based on changes in best practice valuation guidelines and practices

  • Help prepare any required OTPP board reporting with respect to any recommended policy changes

  • Help with responding to queries/information requests from OTPP’s external auditors with respect to private investment valuations

  • Communicate noted issues with respect to the valuation process or specific private investment valuations on a timely basis to management within Finance

  • Build rapport and work cross-functionally with members of other OTPP groups, including Risk Analytics, Tax, Financial and Management Reporting, Private Asset Operations, Strategy and Risk, and Data Management in sharing information generated and reviewed by the valuation team

  • Assist with the preparation of OTPP board reporting with respect to the results of the year end private investment valuation process

  • Contribute to ad-hoc projects supporting the execution of the broader Finance division strategy

What you'll need

  • Relevant university degree.

  • Achieved or working towards a professional accounting designation (“CPA”), and/or Chartered Business Valuator (“CBV”) or Chartered Financial Analyst (“CFA”).

  • Valuation Knowledge – This position requires a solid understanding and application of the principles and complexities of private asset valuation.

  • Analytical Thinking and Financial Modeling Skills – Strong analytical thinking, variance analysis, and financial/cash flow modeling skills are important to the execution of the valuation review process. Having a comfort level in reviewing large and complex financial models is important.

  • Organization Skills – OTPP’s valuation process involves the completion of an extensive number of valuation reviews and coordination across multiple departments and teams including within Finance. The position requires effective organizational skills to set priorities, coordinate tasks and meet deadlines.

  • Written Communication Skills – This position requires the preparation of written memoranda supported by in-depth valuation analyses. Strong written communication skills are required to ensure Finance’s memoranda are written clearly and concisely, address the key valuation risks identified and adequately support Finance’s recommendation of fair value

  • Verbal Communication Skills – This position requires interaction with internal senior management and various OTPP Investment Division teams for the purpose of completing the valuation review. The nature of the relationship with these parties requires a high degree of professionalism.

  • Experience (3 years) working with private investments, which could include valuing and/or conducting due diligence in respect of private investments.

  • Experience interacting with senior management and other external advisors is an asset

What we’re offering

Pay-for-performance with base salary and annual incentive based on individual and enterprise performance. The Base Salary range for this level is as follows: $105,000 - $155,000. Individual compensation and placement of this role in the range is based on different factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, demonstrated competencies, and internal equity as they relate to the role.

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Senior Manager, Valuation
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Senior Manager, Valuation will be responsible for the execution of OTPP’s semi-annual private investment valuation processes, for the purpose of OTPP’s mid-year and year-end financial statement reporting. Private portfolio investments asset classes include private equity, infrastructure, natural resources, venture capital, real estate, and private credit. This position will also actively involve training and reviewing the work of junior staff.

Who you'll work with

The Senior Manager will report to the Director, Valuation.

What you'll do

Leading the planning and execution of the mid-year and year-end valuation processes during peak periods, including:

  • Prepare and oversee the review of “fair value” valuations prepared by OTPP’s Investment teams including the preparation of analysis and summary memorandums in respect thereof

  • Developing an appropriate understanding of the assigned investments and remaining current on individual investment developments through participation in Investment team portfolio monitoring meetings

  • Review the fair value valuations prepared by external valuation agents

  • Develop recommendations of fair value to be used for the purposes of OTPP’s financial statements

  • Support the Director, Valuation in the development, tracking and reporting of plans and timelines to ensure an effective and efficient valuation process

  • Develop the standards for reviews and the corresponding processes to be followed by the staff involved in the valuation process

  • Respond to queries/information requests from OTPP’s external auditors with respect to the private investment valuations

  • Communicate noted issues and generate solutions with respect to the valuation process or specific investment valuations to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

Interface with the Investment Division with respect to the valuations, including:

  • Coordinating processes and deliverables to facilitate the execution of a smooth mid-year and year-end valuation process

  • Communicating noted issues with respect to the process and/or issues identified with respect to specific investment valuations

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided to the Investment Division with respect to private investment valuations 

  • Responding to inquiries and requests from the Investment Division including consultation on new transactions where required

Managing external advisor involvement in the mid-year and year-end valuation process, including:

  • Develop recommendations on the nature and use of external advisors including the selection of specific advisors and the scope of their review

  • Managing the entire process around the use of external advisors including:

  • Execution of terms of engagement with specific advisors

  • Establishing timelines, deliverables and reporting processes for external advisors

  • Ensuring internal information required by external advisors is provided by both the Investment Division and Finance in accordance with established timelines

  • Monitoring and reporting on progress of external advisor deliverables and associated costs

  • Review and incorporation of external advisor recommendations/findings into OTPP’s recommendations on fair value

  • Communicating noted issues, and generating solutions, to the Director, Valuation on a timely basis

  • Ensuring exceptional service is provided by external parties

Training, coaching and Supervision of junior staff, including:

  • Be involved in the hiring of junior staff

  • Develop training material for junior staff

  • Provide on the job coaching and train junior staff with respect to valuation standards, practices, and internal process requirements to ensure the execution of effective and efficient valuation processes

Ensure OTPP’s valuation policies and practices remain best practice, including:

  • Review and monitoring of external best practice valuation standards and practices

  • Be involved in the annual review and update of OTPP’s valuation policies including recommending changes based on changes in best practice valuation guidelines and practices

  • Prepare any required OTPP Board reporting with respect to any recommended process or policy changes

  • Contribute to OTPP Board reporting with respect to the results of the mid-year and year end private investment valuation processes

  • Contribute to ad-hoc projects supporting the execution of the broader Finance division strategy

What you'll need

  • Relevant university degree

  • A professional accounting designation (“CPA”), a Chartered Financial Analyst (“CFA”) and/or Chartered Business Valuator (“CBV”) designation

  • Valuation Knowledge – This position requires a solid understanding and application of the principles and complexities of private asset valuation. This includes experience in the application of various valuation approaches as it might relate to private equity, infrastructure, venture capital, natural resources, real estate, private debt, and other private investments.

  • Analytical Thinking and Financial Modelling Skills – Strong analytical thinking, variance analysis, and financial/cash flow modelling skills are important to the execution of the valuation review process.  Having a comfort level in reviewing large and complex financial models is important. 

  • Project Management Skills – OTPP’s valuation process involves the completion of an extensive number of valuation reviews and coordination across multiple departments and teams including within Finance.  Effective planning and project management skills to set priorities, coordinate tasks, supervise junior staff and meet deadlines are essential.

  • Written Communication Skills – This position requires the preparation of written memoranda supported by in-depth valuation analyses.  Strong written communication skills are required to ensure Finance’s memoranda are written clearly and concisely, address the key valuation risks identified and adequately support Finance’s recommendation of fair value.

  • Verbal Communication Skills – This position requires interaction with internal management and various OTPP Investment Division teams for the purpose of completing the valuation review. The nature of the relationship with these parties requires a high degree of professionalism.

  • Experience in private investment valuations (minimum 8 years)

  • Previous experience in coaching, training and developing staff

  • Experience interacting with senior management, external advisors and external auditors is critical

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Teachers' Venture Growth
jobs.description:
Investment Associate, Teachers' Venture Growth

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan(“Teachers”) is one of Canada’s largest institutional investors. Established as an independent organization in 1990, Teachers’ has built an internationally recognized reputation for innovation and leadership in active investment management. Today, Teachers’ manages more than C$260B in capital on behalf of 340K teachers in the province of Ontario, Canada. Notably, 80% of Teachers’ AUM is managed directly by more than 400 investment professionals operating from offices based in Toronto, San Francisco, London, Singapore and Mumbai.

Teachers’ Venture Growth (“TVG”)is the investment department within OTPP focused on late-stage venture and growth equity investments in disruptive and innovative businesses within the tech ecosystem. Established in 2019, TVG has grown its global investment portfolio to C$10.0B having invested in some of the leading companies in the AI, Cyber, Fintech, and Software sectors. Our global portfolio includes category leaders such as Anthropocene, Databricks, Wayze, Kraken, Quantexa, DeepL, Alan, ComplyAdvantage, Lendable, and Taxfix. TVG’s strategy involves both direct and fund investments and operates with a global team of over 30 professionals.

TVG would typically lead financing rounds and take an active role in closely supporting its portfolio companies, taking board seats, and providing an array of added-value services.

The Opportunity

TVG is looking for an Associate to join the EMEA team, based in London. As a key member of the team, you will contribute to the development and implementation of TVG’s ambitious Europeandirect investment strategy, while gaining exposure to global deal flow.

The Opportunity

TVG is looking for an Associate to join the EMEA team, based in London. As a key member of the team, you will contribute to the development and implementation of TVG’s ambitious European direct investment strategy, while gaining exposure to global deal flow.

What You’ll Do

  • Support origination, due diligence, and execution of investment opportunities across a wide range of sectors within the tech ecosystem in EMEA
  • Perform financial modelling and analysis (financial projections, unit economics analysis, sensitivity analysis, valuations, and returns)
  • Support investment recommendations based on the results of the team’s due diligence findings and analysis
  • Prepare and present investment recommendations and memos to internal committees
  • Evaluate and monitor the ongoing performance of portfolio company investments
  • Proactively add value and build relationships with portfolio companies
  • Establish and build strong relationships within the venture capital ecosystem, including with start-ups, venture funds, corporate partners, and other key stakeholders. You will be able to work and interact with entrepreneurs and executive teams from some of the best high growth technology companies
  • Work cross-functionally and collaboratively with other TVG teams across North America and Asia to ensure continued alignment with Ontario Teachers’ mission, vision, and values

What you’ll Bring

  • Strong academic credentials with a bachelors degree or other relevance academic background
  • 2-3 years of relevant industry experience (preferably in investment banking, venture capital, private equity, or growth equity)
  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth analysis
  • Exceptionally curious and driven, with the ability to balance competing priorities, work independently and thrive in a high performance environment
  • Strong work ethic, team mindset, communication and presentation skills
  • Team player with strong interpersonal and relationship building skills, able to build partnerships internally and externally
  • A risk taker and comfortable with ambiguity but able to balance increased risk taking with strategic and operational judgement
  • Demonstrate ability to think independently
  • High proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Proficiency in English is required, other European languages are highly preferred
  • Ability and willingness to travel, as required

What we're offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

jobs.title: Associate, Portfolio Solutions – Equities
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We are looking for an Associate, Portfolio Solutions - Equities to help shape how our global Private Equity portfolio is measured, managed, and positioned across the organization. In this role, you will work at the intersection of portfolio analytics, valuation, data strategy, and investment insights, contributing to decisions that support our long-term investment strategy.

If you are energized by solving complex analytical challenges, building scalable reporting frameworks, and partnering with private market investors and enterprise teams, this is an opportunity to make an immediate and meaningful impact.

Who You Will Work With

You will report to the Director, Portfolio Solutions – Equities and collaborate closely with colleagues across the Private Equity investment and strategy teams, the broader Portfolio Solutions Group (PSG), and senior leaders in Finance, Risk, Total Fund Management, and the CIO Office.


This is a highly collaborative role where you willl build trusted relationships across the enterprise and become a key connector between investment teams and core governance functions.

What You Will Do

Portfolio Performance Measurement, Construction & Strategy

  • Produce analytics and insights that inform portfolio construction, asset allocation, exposure management, and performance evaluation.

  • Enhance portfolio reporting and dashboards to improve visibility into portfolio exposures and performance drivers.

  • Translate complex analytical findings into clear, decision‑ready narratives for senior leadership.

Data Strategy & Modernization

  • Play a foundational role in modernizing our private asset data infrastructure by building scalable processes, improving data quality, and supporting automation of reporting.

  • Manage key data workflows, including quarterly financials, forecast updates, portfolio review materials, and other recurring deliverables alongside investment teams.

  • Support the development and execution of the Private Equity data roadmap, including data quality improvements, reporting automation, and scalable analytics.

Valuation

  • Coordinate core components of our semi‑annual Private Equity valuation process.

  • Review materials for accuracy and consistency; prepare summaries and insights for leadership.

  • Support high‑quality, timely valuation deliverables across the portfolio to interpret outcomes for management decision-making

Cross‑Functional Partnership & Reporting

  • Serve as a liaison between Private Equity and Finance, Risk, Legal, Compliance, TFM, and the CIO Office to support effective governance and portfolio reporting.

  • Lead and contribute to operating model improvements, cross‑asset‑class initiatives, and strategic PSG projects.

  • Prepare high‑quality materials for senior leadership committees and Board‑level reporting.

  • Support ad hoc analysis and stakeholder requests with accuracy and attention to detail.

What You Will Need

  • Strong analytical and financial modelling capabilities, with the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate insights into recommendations.

  • Understanding of private asset valuation methodologies and key risk concepts.

  • Exceptional communication and presentation skills, including the ability to create executive‑ready materials.

  • Proven ability to manage multiple deliverables in a fast‑paced environment with strong ownership and attention to detail.

  • Experience collaborating across functions and influencing stakeholders.

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, particularly Excel, PowerPoint, and PowerBI.

  • A degree in accounting, business, finance, economics, or a related field or equivalent experience.

  • Experience in investment banking, consulting, valuation, accounting / advisory, or a similar high‑performance environment.

  • Exposure to private markets and understanding of SQL

  • Professional designations (e.g., CFA, CPA, CBV) are assets

  • Knowledge of private asset workflow management tools is an asset

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Director, Hedge Funds
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced investment leader to join our Hedge Funds & ILS (Insurance‑Linked Securities) team as a Director, Hedge Funds. This role offers a compelling opportunity to make an immediate impact on a large, sophisticated hedge fund platform while helping shape its continued evolution as market environments and opportunity sets change.

The Hedge Funds & ILS team manages over $11 billion in capital allocated to external managers across a broad range of strategies and global markets. As part of the Capital Markets department, the team plays a critical role in generating enhanced, diversified returns by partnering with external managers who bring differentiated expertise that complements Ontario Teachers’ internal investment capabilities.

This role is ideal for a senior investor with deep experience in Rates and Macro strategies, strong risk‑taking judgment, and the ability to lead portfolio decisions in dynamic and uncertain market conditions.

Who You’ll Work With

The Hedge Funds & ILS team consists of approximately 10 investment professionals based in Toronto and London, with a mandate to deliver absolute, uncorrelated returns that enhance diversification across the broader portfolio.

You will report directly to the Managing Director, Hedge Funds & ILS, and work within a collaborative decision‑making framework governing portfolio construction, capital allocation, and risk management. As part of a dynamic trading‑floor environment, you will interact daily with colleagues across Capital Markets, leveraging the expertise of more than 100 investment professionals across asset classes and regions.

What You’ll Do

  • Play a key role in the active management of the total hedge fund portfolio, with primary responsibility for strategic thinking and positioning within Rates and Macro strategies.

  • Be directly accountable for the performance and risk management of a portfolio of external hedge fund managers across strategies including Rates Relative Value, Macro, and Multi‑Strategy.

  • Build and leverage your network of relationships to lead the origination of new managers that are accretive to the portfolio

  • Develop and maintain a forward‑looking view of market opportunity sets, adjusting portfolio positioning as market environments evolve.

  • Lead comprehensive investment and operational due diligence on external hedge fund managers, including negotiation of investment terms and coordination with internal partners across legal, compliance, risk, and operations.

  • Monitor portfolio exposures, risk metrics, and P&L across individual managers, strategies, and the overall hedge fund portfolio.

  • Contribute to the development of analytical tools and frameworks that support portfolio construction, risk analysis, and tactical investment decisions.

  • Provide mentorship, guidance, and performance feedback to junior team members, fostering a collaborative and high‑performing investment culture.

  • Contribute to the long‑term vision and strategic direction of the Hedge Funds & ILS platform.

  • Build effective partnerships across Capital Markets and the broader organization to advance shared investment objectives.

What You’ll Bring

  • A minimum of 8 years of relevant experience within an investment bank, asset manager, hedge fund, or fund‑of‑funds environment, ideally with direct exposure to portfolio management and risk taking.

  • Deep expertise in Rates / Fixed Income and Macro strategies, both as asset classes and investment approaches.

  • Proven ability to make risk‑adjusted investment decisions under uncertainty, balancing opportunity, precision, and timing.

  • Broad understanding of the hedge fund landscape, including hedge fund strategies, portfolio theory, valuation, and risk management.

  • Demonstrated investment track record with prior experience leading and developing junior staff.

  • Strong quantitative, analytical, and modeling capabilities to assess risk and return drivers.

  • Undergraduate or postgraduate degree in a relevant discipline such as economics, finance, business, engineering, or computer science, or equivalent experience

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey complex investment concepts to diverse audiences.

  • Strong negotiation and relationship‑management skills.

  • Willingness to travel globally as required.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Sustainable Investing - Climate and Energy Transition
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Associate, Sustainable Investing (Climate and Energy Transition) is an entrepreneurial and analytically driven professional who is passionate about the opportunity to help shape the low-carbon transition at scale. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and real-world execution, they will work directly with portfolio companies and internal partners to accelerate decarbonization, strengthen climate resilience, and support long-term value creation for the fund.

As a core member of one of Canada’s leading Sustainable Investing teams, the Associate will contribute to fund-level climate initiatives while engaging deeply with portfolio companies to design and advance credible transition and decarbonization plans. The Associate will collaborate across teams to assess systemic climate risks and concentrations, support ESG due diligence for private investments, contribute research and analytics to stewardship and climate strategies, and help mobilize fund-wide climate programs.

This is a highly impactful opportunity for a professional who combines quantitative rigor, intellectual curiosity, and strong interpersonal skills—and who is eager to help drive meaningful progress in the global low-carbon transition

Who you’ll work with

  • The Sustainable Investing (SI) team has 15 members and works across the organization to ensure that the Fund is effectively managing sustainability related risks and opportunities across the investment lifecycle

  • The Associate will report to the Director, Climate and Energy Transition, who is primarily responsible for implementing our work with private investments across all sectors, including Real Estate, Infrastructure, Natural Resources, Private Equity, and Teachers’ Venture Growth, to evaluate and implement climate transition plans and strategies

  • Given the cross-departmental nature of the Sustainable Investing team, the Associate will interact on a frequent basis with colleagues from various divisions and teams across the fund, especially the private investment departments

What you’ll do

  • Work with other SI team members on a range of fund-level climate change initiatives.

  • Work with portfolio companies to establish transition and/or decarbonization plans – will include evaluating and identifying operational programs or capital project opportunities, contributing to capital planning processes and discussions, providing support for business cases, and supporting implementation as needed

  • Provide expertise in emissions management and monitoring

  • Keep abreast of developments in the climate change space, including policy and regulatory changes, evolving GHG accounting standards, and industry initiatives

  • Support ESG due diligence for private deals – desktop research, review of consultant reports, participation in meetings with deal teams and consultants

  • Support cross–asset class analysis of systemic risk drivers and concentrations using top-down and bottom-up approaches, including the application of climate risk tools and environmental data to assess physical and transition risks

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders in the execution of fund-wide climate change programs and initiatives

  • Provide research, analytics and communication materials to support any of the evolving climate change, stewardship and social strategies across SI

  • Support other related initiatives as required

What you’ll need

  • Minimum Bachelor; Masters degree in engineering, environment, climate science, finance or other relevant field an asset

  • Completion of the CFA (or underway) would be an asset but not required

  • Knowledge of and passion for sustainable investing, the energy transition and addressing climate change

  • Experience working on decarbonization plans

  • Quantitative modelling skillset (excel, python, matlab, etc.) an asset

  • Strong interpersonal, communication and presentation skills (both oral and written) – particularly an ability to understand and communicate technical climate and sustainability material to investment professionals and company management

  • Familiarity with investment-related concepts, such as valuation models, capital markets and fundamental company analysis an asset

  • Works well independently or as a member of a team

  • Highly adaptable, flexible and comfortable with ambiguity

  • Ability to manage multiple and changing priorities

  • 2-5 years related experience in energy, decarbonization, sustainability, or climate consulting. Direct experience in asset management or value creation with climate change/decarbonization focus an asset.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Managing Director, Private Equity - Software
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Managing Director will be working with investment professionals with deep sector and regional expertise and will play a key role in making and managing direct investments in the Technology sector.

Who you'll will work with 

Reporting to the Senior Managing Director (SMD), the Managing Director will lead deal teams comprising analysts, associates and principals, supported by third party consultants, operating executives, legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular investment.

The individual will have significant prior expertise and a strong track record investing in Technology, particularly in Software with knowledge of emerging AI and digitization themes.  Key focus areas include Application software, Infrastructure software, Digital & IT Services and other Tech-enabled categories.

In certain instances, the Managing Director may also work with colleagues in the London, Singapore or Mumbai offices in evaluating international investment opportunities in a given sector.

What you'll do

  • When evaluating new investments, the Managing Director will lead, with oversight of SMD, a small team determining whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. Among other things, the analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company due diligence, creating sophisticated financial models, arranging debt financing, negotiating legal documents, formulating value creation plans (in partnership with operating teams and other lead investors) and preparing written recommendations.

  • Once a transaction has been completed, the Managing Director will remain involved with the business, and will work with our investment partners and the management team to implement value-enhancing initiatives. The Managing Director may be expected to take lead roles at the board and/or sub-committees of the board in some cases. 

  • They will play an active role in origination, identifying target investments and creating a “right to win”, which should include unique value creation levers.  Activities will include conducting desk research, working with consultants, operating executives, attending conferences and trade shows, networking with management teams and meeting with various industry participants. This will require the individual to build high-impact relationships and networks across the Technology investing ecosystem.

  • They will lead sub-sector research and deep dives as the team evolves its coverage strategy and focus areas over time with shifting industry dynamics such as the emergence of AI impacting investment decisions.

  • Collaborate with other Global Sector teams and across Investments on specific departmental initiatives.

  • Seek to lead deals with input and high level of communication from global sector SMD.

What you'll need

  • 15+ years relevant work experience, ideally the most recent experience leading deals in Technology private equity.  Active Technology M&A in a lead role in corporate development would also be considered. Supplemented by previous experiences in management consulting, investment banking and/or corporations (preferably corporate development or similar roles).

  • Tenured private equity investor with a strong track record (technology/software experience strongly preferred). Please include deal sheets with your application.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, leading multiple diligence streams such as financial, operational, legal, tax, insurance as well as arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Significant experience serving on Boards of Directors

  • Established network of industry professionals, including mid to bulge bracket private equity, senior executives, and advisors.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Strong negotiation, collaboration and communication skills.

  • An ability to balance priorities, work independently, lead projects and thrive in a high performance team environment.

  • Willingness and ability to travel globally.

#LI-EO1

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

At Ontario Teachers', diversity is one of our core strengths. We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring.  We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves.  To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

How to apply

Are you ready to pursue new challenges and take your career to the next level? Apply today! You may be invited to complete a pre-recorded digital interview as part of your application.

Accommodations are available upon request (peopleandculture@otpp.com) for candidates with a disability taking part in the recruitment process and once hired.

Candidates must be legally entitled to work in the country where this role is located.

Ontario Teachers’ may use AI-based tools to assist in screening and assessing applicants for this position. These tools may help us identify candidates whose skills and experience align with Ontario Teachers’ objectives by analyzing information provided in resumes and applications. Our use of AI does not replace human decision-making.

To learn more about how Teachers’ uses AI with your personal information, please visit our Privacy Centre.

companyDetails.description: The Managing Director will be working with investment professionals with deep sector and regional expertise and will play a key role in making and managing direct investments in the Technology sector.

jobs.title: Business Development, Associate
jobs.description:
About TPG

TPG is a leading global alternative asset management firm, founded in San Francisco in 1992, with investment and operational teams around the world. TPG invests across a broadly diversified set of strategies, including private equity, impact, credit, real estate, and market solutions, and our unique strategy is driven by collaboration, innovation, and inclusion. Our teams combine deep product and sector experience with broad capabilities and expertise to develop differentiated insights and add value for our fund investors, portfolio companies, management teams, and communities.

TPG’s success depends on our people, and we build and sustain our world-class team by creating an inclusive, supportive culture within the firm that seeks excellence and encourages humility and transparency. The quality of our investments and our ability to build great companies depend on the originality of our insights. Reaching our firm’s full potential means supporting every team member to bring the fullness of their unique perspective to their work and to our community. We are committed to a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace to foster diversity of thought and reflect the breadth of our limited partners and portfolio companies.

Description of Position

The Associate will be responsible for supporting strategic investments and transactions to drive organic and inorganic growth for TPG. This is an opportunity to join a lean and entrepreneurial team with the mandate to acquire, create and grow strategically critical businesses for TPG through investments with a wide range of complexity and structures. This position will support the identification and evaluation of new investment and growth opportunities for TPG through private equity-style underwriting and transaction execution with a focus on financial services. Additionally, the role will offer a unique experience to invest across various transaction types including majority acquisitions, structured minority investments and strategic partnerships. The position is highly cross-functional and distinct from traditional private equity roles, with significant exposure to TPG’s executive office and collaboration with investment teams across the firm. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest and background in building complex financial models, conducting due diligence, drafting investment committee memos, researching company and industry trends, demonstrating excellent written and oral communication and supporting workstreams across all aspects of deal execution.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Perform in-depth research to source and evaluate potential investment opportunities across the financial services sector
  • Assess industry trends and competitive landscapes to provide actionable insights for strategic decision-making to support growth of TPG
  • Execute rigorous financial analysis, including valuation and modeling, to determine the viability of prospective investments
  • Construct sophisticated operating models to forecast financial performance and quantify value creation strategies for potential deals
  • Undertake thorough business due diligence to scrutinize target companies' business models, operations, financial stability and growth trajectories
  • Develop comprehensive due diligence materials that integrate quantitative analytics, market research, and essential background data
  • Engage with industry experts and management teams to extract critical insights and validate core investment hypotheses
  • Support due diligence across commercial, financial and tax workstreams helping manage internal and external stakeholders
  • Synthesize findings into presentations and written summaries to communicate investment theses effectively to investment committees
  • Produce comprehensive investment memoranda tailored for both internal leadership and external stakeholders

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree and above, with an exceptional record of academic achievement
  • 2+ years of professional experience in investment banking and/or private equity
  • Robust understanding of accounting and finance and proficiency in financial modeling and analysis
  • Excellent analytical and critical thinking skills with strong attention to detail
  • Proactive, motivated, and organized; ability to multi-task and prioritize in a demanding environment with a dynamic pace; exceptional time, organizational, and project management skills
  • Outstanding communication skills; combination of verbal, written, and presentation skills, with the ability to articulate messaging effectively and express complex information concisely
  • Ability to coordinate and build relationships across multiple internal groups

It is expected that the annual base salary for this role will range from $130,000 – $165,000. Base salary is one component of TPG’s competitive total rewards package which typically includes discretionary compensation, comprehensive health benefits, and paid time off. Other incentives may also be available to certain eligible employees.

jobs.title: Search Fund Intern (Deal Sourcing / Investment Analysis)
jobs.description: Unpaid. Full-time or part-time Spring/Summer/Fall. Principals in NY and LA. Deal sourcing, market research, investment analysis.

jobs.title: M&A Research Intern
jobs.description: Buy-side M&A advisory. Business development and research teams. Message with keyword "dealflow" to apply.

jobs.title: Deal Sourcing & Investment Intern (Summer 2026)
jobs.description: Independent sponsor based in Montreal. Deal sourcing, cold outreach, screening, diligence. Performance-based upside. Apply: https://tally.so/r/KYLzVX

jobs.title: Investment Analyst / Associate Intern 2026
jobs.description: Unpaid. 15-20 hrs/week. Two periods: Apr 6-Jun 6 and Jun 6-Aug 31 2026. $10K bonus if sourced deal closes. Full acquisition lifecycle exposure.

jobs.title: Deal Sourcing Internship (Summer 2026)
jobs.description: Early-stage PE firm, EV $3M-$10M targets. Remote (US only). Rising juniors/seniors or first-year grad students. Apply: https://lighthouseequitypartners.com/internships/

jobs.title: Private Equity Internship (Research / BDA)
jobs.description: Unpaid 16-week program with path to paid role and FT offer. ESG-focused PE fund. Apply: Recruiting@capraecapital.com with completed Caprae Handbook.

jobs.title: Private Equity Internship (Undergrad)
jobs.description: Unpaid, course credit required, 20+ hrs/week, 12+ weeks. Work directly with Searcher-CEOs. MBA internship also available (paid, 8-10 weeks).

jobs.title: M&A Intern - Summer 2026
jobs.description: Hands-on M&A internship combining outbound client sourcing, deal research, and financial analysis. Must be Western Canada based. Complete details and application instructions available in attached PDF.

jobs.title: Prospecting and Sales Support Intern (Spring 2026 Semester)
jobs.description: Support acquisition finance prospecting and sales efforts by identifying and researching entrepreneurs, maintaining CRM data, and conducting initial diligence. $15/hour, ~20 hours/week, January 2026 start; application details not provided in post.

jobs.title: Buyside Intern (Paid)
jobs.description: High-intensity M&A training internship (10 hrs/week) focusing on founder outreach, deal structuring, and negotiation fundamentals in lower-middle-market private equity. Paid position with strong conversion track record to full-time roles.

jobs.title: Business Internship
jobs.description: Leadership internship role in news, media, and marketing sectors focused on startups and small acquisitions with responsibilities spanning business development, sales, operations, and strategic decisions. Compensation includes stock options for select candidates; apply via Google Form.

jobs.title: Sales Development & Executive Mentorship Internship
jobs.description: Sales outreach internship focused on home builder prospecting under direct mentorship of CEO Salvador Herrera. No compensation details or application instructions provided in the posting.
